        OR REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.name), r'^(.*(([^a-df-z]|^)shop|shopping).*)$')
        AND REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.medium), r'^(.*cp.*|ppc|retargeting|paid.*)$') THEN 'Paid Shopping'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('baidu', 'bing', 'duckduckgo', 'ecosia', 'google', 'yahoo', 'yandex')
        AND REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.medium), r'^(.*cp.*|ppc|paid.*)$') THEN 'Paid Search'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('badoo', 'facebook', 'fb', 'instagram', 'linkedin', 'pinterest', 'tiktok', 'twitter', 'whatsapp')
        AND REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.medium), r'^(.*cp.*|ppc|retargeting|paid.*)$') THEN 'Paid Social'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('dailymotion', 'disneyplus', 'netflix', 'youtube', 'vimeo', 'twitch', 'vimeo', 'youtube')
        AND REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.medium), r'^(.*cp.*|ppc|retargeting|paid.*)$') THEN 'Paid Video'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.medium) IN ('display', 'banner', 'expandable', 'interstitial', 'cpm') THEN 'Display'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('alibaba', 'amazon', 'google shopping', 'shopify', 'etsy', 'ebay', 'stripe', 'walmart')
        OR REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.name), r'^(.*(([^a-df-z]|^)shop|shopping).*)$') THEN 'Organic Shopping'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('badoo', 'facebook', 'fb', 'instagram', 'linkedin', 'pinterest', 'tiktok', 'twitter', 'whatsapp')
        OR LOWER(traffic_source.medium) IN ('social', 'social-network', 'social-media', 'sm', 'social network', 'social media') THEN 'Organic Social'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('dailymotion', 'disneyplus', 'netflix', 'youtube', 'vimeo', 'twitch', 'vimeo', 'youtube')
        OR REGEXP_CONTAINS(LOWER(traffic_source.medium), r'^(.*video.*)$') THEN 'Organic Video'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('baidu', 'bing', 'duckduckgo', 'ecosia', 'google', 'yahoo', 'yandex')
        OR LOWER(traffic_source.medium) = 'organic' THEN 'Organic Search'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.medium) = 'referral' THEN 'Referral'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) IN ('email', 'e-mail', 'e_mail', 'e mail')
        OR LOWER(traffic_source.medium) IN ('email', 'e-mail', 'e_mail', 'e mail') THEN 'Email'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.medium) = 'affiliate' THEN 'Affiliates'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.medium) = 'audio' THEN 'Audio'
      WHEN LOWER(traffic_source.source) = 'sms' OR LOWER(traffic_source.medium) = 'sms' THEN 'SMS'
      ELSE 'Unassigned'
    END AS channel_group,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CONCAT(user_pseudo_id, CAST(event_timestamp AS STRING))) AS sessions
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce.events_*`
  WHERE _TABLE_SUFFIX BETWEEN '20201201' AND '20201231'
    AND event_name = 'session_start'
  GROUP BY channel_group
)
SELECT channel_group, sessions
FROM session_channel
ORDER BY sessions DESC
LIMIT 4

WITH RECURSIVE
dates AS (
    SELECT DATE('2017-01-01') AS dt
    UNION ALL
    SELECT DATE(dt, '+1 day')
    FROM dates
    WHERE dt < '2018-08-29'
),
daily_sales AS (
    SELECT d.dt AS sale_date,
           COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.product_category_name_english = 'toys' THEN oi.price ELSE 0 END), 0) AS total_sales
    FROM dates d
    LEFT JOIN orders o ON DATE(o.order_purchase_timestamp) = d.dt
    LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    LEFT JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    LEFT JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name
    GROUP BY d.dt
),
stats AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS n,
           SUM(day_num) AS sum_x,
           SUM(total_sales) AS sum_y,
           SUM(day_num * total_sales) AS sum_xy,
           SUM(day_num * day_num) AS sum_xx
    FROM (
        SELECT total_sales,
               julianday(sale_date) - julianday('2017-01-01') AS day_num
        FROM daily_sales
    )
),
coeff AS (
    SELECT (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) AS slope,
           (sum_y - ((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x)) * sum_x) / n AS intercept
    FROM stats
),
prediction_dates AS (
    SELECT DATE('2018-12-03') AS dt
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-04'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-05'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-06'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-07'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-08'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-09'
    UNION ALL SELECT '2018-12-10'
),
predicted_sales AS (
    SELECT dt,
           intercept + slope * (julianday(dt) - julianday('2017-01-01')) AS pred_sales
    FROM prediction_dates, coeff
),
moving_averages AS (
    SELECT p1.dt AS target_date,
           (SELECT AVG(p2.pred_sales)
            FROM predicted_sales p2
            WHERE julianday(p2.dt) BETWEEN julianday(p1.dt) - 2 AND julianday(p1.dt) + 2
           ) AS ma_5_symmetric
    FROM predicted_sales p1
    WHERE p1.dt IN ('2018-12-05', '2018-12-06', '2018-12-07', '2018-12-08')
)
SELECT SUM(ma_5_symmetric) AS sum_of_moving_averages
FROM moving_averages
pred.local003:
-- Step 1: Define reference date for recency calculation (latest order purchase timestamp among delivered orders)
WITH reference_date AS (
    SELECT MAX(order_purchase_timestamp) AS max_date
    FROM orders
    WHERE order_status = 'delivered'
),
-- Step 2: Customer-level metrics (only delivered orders)
customer_metrics AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_unique_id,
        MAX(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_purchase_date,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders,
        SUM(op.payment_value) AS total_spend
    FROM orders o
    JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id = op.order_id
    JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'
    GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id
),
-- Step 3: Add recency in days and average sales per order
customer_rfm_raw AS (
    SELECT
        cm.customer_unique_id,
        cm.total_orders,
        cm.total_spend,
        cm.total_spend / cm.total_orders AS avg_sales_per_order,
        (SELECT MAX(julianday(max_date)) - julianday(cm.last_purchase_date) FROM reference_date) AS recency_days
    FROM customer_metrics cm
),
-- Step 4: Assign RFM scores using quintiles (1 = best, 5 = worst)
rfm_scores AS (
    SELECT
        customer_unique_id,
        total_orders,
        total_spend,
        avg_sales_per_order,
        recency_days,
        -- R score: lower recency is better (most recent = 1)
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY recency_days ASC) AS r_score,
        -- F score: higher frequency is better
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY total_orders DESC) AS f_score,
        -- M score: higher monetary is better
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY total_spend DESC) AS m_score
    FROM customer_rfm_raw
),
-- Step 5: Create RFM segment label
rfm_segments AS (
    SELECT
        customer_unique_id,
        avg_sales_per_order,
        r_score || '-' || f_score || '-' || m_score AS rfm_segment
    FROM rfm_scores
)
-- Step 6: Report average sales per order per segment
SELECT
    rfm_segment,
    ROUND(AVG(avg_sales_per_order), 2) AS avg_sales_per_order_segment,
    COUNT(*) AS customer_count
FROM rfm_segments
GROUP BY rfm_segment
ORDER BY rfm_segment
pred.local004:
WITH customer_metrics AS (
    SELECT 
        o.customer_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS num_orders,
        SUM(op.payment_value) AS total_payment,
        MIN(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS first_purchase,
        MAX(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_purchase
    FROM orders o
    INNER JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id = op.order_id
    GROUP BY o.customer_id
),
customer_avg AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        num_orders,
        total_payment / num_orders AS avg_payment_per_order,
        CASE 
            WHEN (julianday(last_purchase) - julianday(first_purchase)) < 7 
            THEN 1.0 
            ELSE (julianday(last_purchase) - julianday(first_purchase)) / 7.0 
        END AS lifespan_weeks
    FROM customer_metrics
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        num_orders,
        avg_payment_per_order,
        lifespan_weeks,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_payment_per_order DESC) AS rn
    FROM customer_avg
)
SELECT 
    customer_id,
    num_orders AS number_of_orders,
    avg_payment_per_order AS average_payment_per_order,
    lifespan_weeks AS customer_lifespan_in_weeks
FROM ranked
WHERE rn <= 3
ORDER BY avg_payment_per_order DESC

WITH dates AS (
  SELECT player_id,
         CASE WHEN debut <= final_game THEN debut ELSE final_game END AS d1,
         CASE WHEN debut <= final_game THEN final_game ELSE debut END AS d2
  FROM player
  WHERE debut IS NOT NULL AND final_game IS NOT NULL
),
years_calc AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2,
         CAST(strftime('%Y', d2) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', d1) AS INTEGER) -
         CASE WHEN strftime('%m%d', d2) < strftime('%m%d', d1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS yr
  FROM dates
),
anniversary AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2, yr,
         date(d1, '+' || yr || ' years') AS anniv
  FROM years_calc
),
months_calc AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2, yr, anniv,
         (CAST(strftime('%m', d2) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%m', anniv) AS INTEGER) -
         CASE WHEN strftime('%d', d2) < strftime('%d', anniv) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + 12) % 12 AS mon
  FROM anniversary
),
month_start AS (
  SELECT player_id, d1, d2, yr, mon,
         date(anniv, '+' || mon || ' months') AS month_start
  FROM months_calc
),
days_calc AS (
  SELECT player_id, yr, mon,
         CAST(julianday(d2) - julianday(month_start) AS INTEGER) AS dy
  FROM month_start
),
span AS (
  SELECT player_id,
         ROUND(CAST(yr AS REAL), 2) + ROUND(CAST(mon AS REAL)/12.0, 2) + ROUND(CAST(dy AS REAL)/365.0, 2) AS career_span
  FROM days_calc
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(career_span), 2) AS avg_career_span
FROM span

WITH
-- Step 1: Get airport code for Abakan
abakan_code AS (
    SELECT a.airport_code
    FROM airports_data a
    WHERE json_extract(a.city, '$.en') = 'Abakan'
       OR json_extract(a.city, '$.ru') = 'Абакан'
),
-- Step 2: Get coordinates for all airports as numeric latitude/longitude
airport_coords AS (
    SELECT
        airport_code,
        CAST(
            SUBSTR(
                coordinates,
                INSTR(coordinates, ',') + 1,
                LENGTH(coordinates) - INSTR(coordinates, ',') - 1
            ) AS REAL
        ) AS latitude,
        CAST(
            SUBSTR(
                coordinates,
                2,
                INSTR(coordinates, ',') - 2
            ) AS REAL
        ) AS longitude
    FROM airports_data
),
-- Step 3: Join flights with departure and arrival airports
flights_with_coords AS (
    SELECT
        f.flight_id,
        f.departure_airport,
        f.arrival_airport,
        d.latitude AS dep_lat,
        d.longitude AS dep_lon,
        a.latitude AS arr_lat,
        a.longitude AS arr_lon
    FROM flights f
    JOIN airport_coords d ON f.departure_airport = d.airport_code
    JOIN airport_coords a ON f.arrival_airport = a.airport_code
    WHERE f.departure_airport IN (SELECT airport_code FROM abakan_code)
       OR f.arrival_airport IN (SELECT airport_code FROM abakan_code)
),
-- Step 4: Compute great-circle distance using Haversine formula (R = 6371 km)
distances AS (
    SELECT
        flight_id,
        6371 * 2 * ASIN(
            SQRT(
                POWER(SIN((RADIANS(arr_lat) - RADIANS(dep_lat)) / 2), 2)
                + COS(RADIANS(dep_lat))
                * COS(RADIANS(arr_lat))
                * POWER(SIN((RADIANS(arr_lon) - RADIANS(dep_lon)) / 2), 2)
            )
        ) AS distance_km
    FROM flights_with_coords
)
-- Step 5: Return the maximum distance
SELECT MAX(distance_km) AS distance_km
FROM distances
pred.local010:
WITH airports AS (
  SELECT 
    airport_code,
    json_extract(city, '$.en') AS city_en,
    CAST(SUBSTR(coordinates, 2, INSTR(coordinates, ',')-2) AS REAL) AS lon,
    CAST(SUBSTR(coordinates, INSTR(coordinates, ',')+1, LENGTH(coordinates)-INSTR(coordinates, ',')-1) AS REAL) AS lat
  FROM airports_data
),
flight_cities AS (
  SELECT 
    f.flight_id,
    dp.city_en AS dep_city,
    dp.lon AS dep_lon,
    dp.lat AS dep_lat,
    ap.city_en AS arr_city,
    ap.lon AS arr_lon,
    ap.lat AS arr_lat
  FROM flights f
  JOIN airports dp ON f.departure_airport = dp.airport_code
  JOIN airports ap ON f.arrival_airport = ap.airport_code
  WHERE dp.city_en != ap.city_en
),
route_distances AS (
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN dep_city < arr_city THEN dep_city ELSE arr_city END AS city1,
    CASE WHEN dep_city < arr_city THEN arr_city ELSE dep_city END AS city2,
    2 * 6371 * ASIN(SQRT(
      POWER(SIN((arr_lat - dep_lat) * 3.141592653589793 / 360), 2) +
      COS(dep_lat * 3.141592653589793 / 180) * COS(arr_lat * 3.141592653589793 / 180) *
      POWER(SIN((arr_lon - dep_lon) * 3.141592653589793 / 360), 2)
    )) AS distance_km
  FROM flight_cities
),
city_pair_avg AS (
  SELECT 
    city1, 
    city2, 
    AVG(distance_km) AS avg_distance
  FROM route_distances
  GROUP BY city1, city2
),
ranges AS (
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN avg_distance < 1000 THEN 0
      WHEN avg_distance < 2000 THEN 1000
      WHEN avg_distance < 3000 THEN 2000
      WHEN avg_distance < 4000 THEN 3000
      WHEN avg_distance < 5000 THEN 4000
      WHEN avg_distance < 6000 THEN 5000
      ELSE 6000
    END AS distance_range,
    COUNT(*) AS pair_count
  FROM city_pair_avg
  GROUP BY distance_range
),
min_count AS (
  SELECT MIN(pair_count) AS min_pair_count
  FROM ranges
)
SELECT pair_count AS answer
FROM ranges, min_count
WHERE ranges.pair_count = min_count.min_pair_count

WITH

mc_collisions AS (

-- Step 2: Get motorcyclist parties (drivers/passengers) with helmet status
motorcyclist_parties AS (
    SELECT

        CASE 
            WHEN (p.party_safety_equipment_1 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet used%' 
               OR p.party_safety_equipment_2 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet used%') THEN 1
            WHEN (p.party_safety_equipment_1 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet not used%' 
               OR p.party_safety_equipment_2 LIKE '%motorcycle helmet not used%') THEN 0
            ELSE NULL
        END AS helmet_used
    FROM parties p
    INNER JOIN mc_collisions mc ON p.case_id = mc.case_id
    WHERE p.party_type IN ('driver', 'passenger')
      AND (p.party_safety_equipment_1 LIKE '%motorcycle%' 
        OR p.party_safety_equipment_2 LIKE '%motorcycle%')
),
-- Step 3: Distinct collisions per helmet group
collisions_with_helmet AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT case_id
    FROM motorcyclist_parties
    WHERE helmet_used = 1
),
collisions_without_helmet AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT case_id
    FROM motorcyclist_parties
    WHERE helmet_used = 0
),
-- Step 4: Motorcyclist fatalities (killed victims matched to motorcyclist parties)
motorcyclist_fatalities AS (
    SELECT v.case_id
    FROM victims v
    INNER JOIN motorcyclist_parties mp 
        ON v.case_id = mp.case_id AND v.party_number = mp.party_number
    WHERE v.victim_degree_of_injury = 'killed'
),
-- Step 5: Aggregate statistics for each helmet group
stats_helmet AS (
    SELECT
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT case_id) FROM collisions_with_helmet) AS total_collisions,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM motorcyclist_fatalities 
         WHERE case_id IN (SELECT case_id FROM collisions_with_helmet)) AS total_fatalities
),
stats_no_helmet AS (
    SELECT
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT case_id) FROM collisions_without_helmet) AS total_collisions,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM motorcyclist_fatalities 
         WHERE case_id IN (SELECT case_id FROM collisions_without_helmet)) AS total_fatalities
)
-- Final output: two percentages rounded to two decimal places
SELECT
    ROUND(100.0 * sh.total_fatalities / NULLIF(sh.total_collisions, 0), 2) AS pct_fatalities_with_helmet,
    ROUND(100.0 * sn.total_fatalities / NULLIF(sn.total_collisions, 0), 2) AS pct_fatalities_without_helmet
FROM stats_helmet sh, stats_no_helmet sn

WITH cause_counts AS (

  GROUP BY year, cause
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    cause,
    cnt,

  FROM cause_counts
),
top_two AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    cause,
    rn
  FROM ranked

),
paired AS (

),
pair_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    pair,
    COUNT(*) AS num_years
  FROM paired
  GROUP BY pair
)

FROM paired

WHERE pair_counts.num_years = 1

category_counts AS (

top_2021 AS (

WITH
-- Step 1: Compute total runs conceded per over per match
per_over AS (
    SELECT
        bo.match_id,
        bo.over_id,
        bo.bowler,
        COALESCE(r.runs, 0) + COALESCE(e.extras, 0) AS total_runs
    FROM (
        SELECT match_id, over_id, MIN(bowler) AS bowler
        FROM ball_by_ball
        GROUP BY match_id, over_id
    ) bo
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT match_id, over_id, SUM(runs_scored) AS runs
        FROM batsman_scored
        GROUP BY match_id, over_id
    ) r ON bo.match_id = r.match_id AND bo.over_id = r.over_id
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT match_id, over_id, SUM(extra_runs) AS extras
        FROM extra_runs
        GROUP BY match_id, over_id
    ) e ON bo.match_id = e.match_id AND bo.over_id = e.over_id
),
-- Step 2: Find the maximum runs per match
match_max AS (
    SELECT match_id, MAX(total_runs) AS max_runs
    FROM per_over
    GROUP BY match_id
),
-- Step 3: Overs that achieved the maximum in their match
top_overs AS (
    SELECT po.*
    FROM per_over po
    JOIN match_max mm ON po.match_id = mm.match_id AND po.total_runs = mm.max_runs
),
-- Step 4: For each bowler, pick the over with the highest runs (if tie, smallest match_id)
bowler_best AS (
    SELECT
        bowler,
        match_id,
        total_runs,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY bowler ORDER BY total_runs DESC, match_id) AS rn
    FROM top_overs
)
-- Step 5: Get the top 3 bowlers by their best over's runs
SELECT
    bowler,
    match_id,
    total_runs AS runs_conceded
FROM bowler_best
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY total_runs DESC
LIMIT 3

WITH bowler_balls AS (
    -- Count total deliveries bowled by each player
    SELECT bowler, COUNT(*) AS balls_bowled
    FROM ball_by_ball
    GROUP BY bowler
),
bowler_wickets AS (
    -- Count total wickets taken by each bowler
    SELECT b.bowler, COUNT(*) AS wickets
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    JOIN wicket_taken w 
      ON b.match_id = w.match_id 
     AND b.over_id = w.over_id 
     AND b.ball_id = w.ball_id 
     AND b.innings_no = w.innings_no
    GROUP BY b.bowler
),
bowler_strike_rate AS (
    -- Compute strike rate (balls per wicket); exclude bowlers with 0 wickets
    SELECT 
        bb.bowler,
        bb.balls_bowled,
        bw.wickets,
        CAST(bb.balls_bowled AS REAL) / bw.wickets AS strike_rate
    FROM bowler_balls bb
    JOIN bowler_wickets bw ON bb.bowler = bw.bowler
    WHERE bw.wickets > 0
)
-- Find the bowler with the lowest strike rate
SELECT p.player_name
FROM bowler_strike_rate bsr
JOIN player p ON bsr.bowler = p.player_id
ORDER BY bsr.strike_rate ASC
LIMIT 1

            AND b.innings_no = s.innings_no
            AND b.over_id = s.over_id

WITH player_runs AS (

        b.striker AS player_id,
        b.match_id,
        SUM(bs.runs_scored) AS total_runs

    JOIN batsman_scored bs 
        ON b.match_id = bs.match_id 
        AND b.innings_no = bs.innings_no 
        AND b.over_id = bs.over_id 
        AND b.ball_id = bs.ball_id
    GROUP BY b.striker, b.match_id
),
runs_100 AS (
    SELECT player_id, match_id
    FROM player_runs
    WHERE total_runs >= 100
),
player_team AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT r.player_id, r.match_id, pm.team_id
    FROM runs_100 r
    JOIN player_match pm ON r.player_id = pm.player_id AND r.match_id = pm.match_id
),
match_info AS (
    SELECT m.match_id, m.match_winner
    FROM match m
    JOIN player_team pt ON m.match_id = pt.match_id
),
losing_players AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT pt.player_id
    FROM player_team pt
    JOIN match_info mi ON pt.match_id = mi.match_id

WITH season5_matches AS (
    SELECT match_id
    FROM match
    WHERE season_id = 5
),
player_runs AS (
    SELECT 
        b.striker AS player_id,
        b.match_id,
        SUM(bs.runs_scored) AS runs_in_match
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    JOIN batsman_scored bs 
        ON b.match_id = bs.match_id 
        AND b.innings_no = bs.innings_no 
        AND b.over_id = bs.over_id 
        AND b.ball_id = bs.ball_id
    WHERE b.match_id IN (SELECT match_id FROM season5_matches)
    GROUP BY b.striker, b.match_id
),
player_total AS (
    SELECT 
        player_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT match_id) AS matches_batted,
        SUM(runs_in_match) AS total_runs
    FROM player_runs
    GROUP BY player_id
),
player_dismissals AS (
    SELECT 
        player_out AS player_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT match_id || '-' || innings_no) AS dismissals
    FROM wicket_taken
    WHERE match_id IN (SELECT match_id FROM season5_matches)
    GROUP BY player_out
),
player_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        p.player_id,
        p.player_name,
        pt.total_runs * 1.0 / NULLIF(pt.matches_batted, 0) AS avg_runs_per_match,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.dismissals IS NULL OR pd.dismissals = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE pt.total_runs * 1.0 / pd.dismissals 
        END AS batting_average
    FROM player p
    LEFT JOIN player_total pt ON p.player_id = pt.player_id
    LEFT JOIN player_dismissals pd ON p.player_id = pd.player_id
    WHERE pt.total_runs IS NOT NULL

SELECT 
    player_name,
    ROUND(avg_runs_per_match, 2) AS avg_runs_per_match,

pred.local025:
WITH extra_per_ball AS (
    -- Sum extra runs per ball (there could be multiple extra types)
    SELECT match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id, SUM(extra_runs) AS extra_runs
    FROM extra_runs
    GROUP BY match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id
),
all_balls AS (
    -- All balls from ball_by_ball, left join bat and extra runs
    SELECT
        b.match_id,
        b.innings_no,
        b.over_id,
        b.ball_id,
        b.bowler,
        COALESCE(bs.runs_scored, 0) AS bat_runs,
        COALESCE(epb.extra_runs, 0) AS extra_runs
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    LEFT JOIN batsman_scored bs
        ON b.match_id = bs.match_id
        AND b.innings_no = bs.innings_no
        AND b.over_id = bs.over_id
        AND b.ball_id = bs.ball_id
    LEFT JOIN extra_per_ball epb
        ON b.match_id = epb.match_id
        AND b.innings_no = epb.innings_no
        AND b.over_id = epb.over_id
        AND b.ball_id = epb.ball_id
),
over_totals AS (
    -- Sum runs per over and get one bowler (all balls in an over share the same bowler)
    SELECT
        match_id,
        innings_no,
        over_id,
        MIN(bowler) AS bowler,  -- any ball's bowler works
        SUM(bat_runs + extra_runs) AS total_runs
    FROM all_balls
    GROUP BY match_id, innings_no, over_id
),
ranked_overs AS (
    -- Rank overs per match (across both innings) by total runs descending
    SELECT
        match_id,
        innings_no,
        over_id,
        bowler,
        total_runs,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY match_id ORDER BY total_runs DESC) AS rn
    FROM over_totals
),
highest_overs AS (
    -- Keep only the single highest over per match
    SELECT
        match_id,
        over_id,
        bowler,
        total_runs
    FROM ranked_overs
    WHERE rn = 1
)
-- Average of per-match highest over totals
SELECT AVG(total_runs) AS avg_highest_over_runs
FROM highest_overs

WITH base AS (

category1 AS (

),
category2 AS (

    FROM base
    GROUP BY seller_id
    ORDER BY val DESC

),
category3 AS (

    FROM base
    GROUP BY seller_id
    ORDER BY val DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
category4 AS (

)

WITH payment_events AS (

    JOIN olist_products pr ON i.product_id = pr.product_id

cat_pay_counts AS (

ranked AS (
    SELECT category,
           payment_type,
           cnt,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS rn
    FROM cat_pay_counts

WITH sorted AS (
    SELECT *,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            ORDER BY geolocation_state, geolocation_city,
                     geolocation_zip_code_prefix, geolocation_lat, geolocation_lng
        ) AS rn
    FROM olist_geolocation
),
with_prev AS (
    SELECT
        s.geolocation_state,
        s.geolocation_city,
        s.geolocation_lat,
        s.geolocation_lng,
        LAG(s.geolocation_state) OVER (ORDER BY s.rn) AS prev_state,
        LAG(s.geolocation_city) OVER (ORDER BY s.rn) AS prev_city,
        LAG(s.geolocation_lat) OVER (ORDER BY s.rn) AS prev_lat,
        LAG(s.geolocation_lng) OVER (ORDER BY s.rn) AS prev_lng
    FROM sorted s
),
with_radians AS (
    SELECT
        prev_city AS city1,
        prev_state AS state1,
        geolocation_city AS city2,
        geolocation_state AS state2,
        prev_lat * 3.14159265358979 / 180 AS lat1_rad,
        prev_lng * 3.14159265358979 / 180 AS lng1_rad,
        geolocation_lat * 3.14159265358979 / 180 AS lat2_rad,
        geolocation_lng * 3.14159265358979 / 180 AS lng2_rad
    FROM with_prev
    WHERE prev_city IS NOT NULL
      AND (prev_city != geolocation_city OR prev_state != geolocation_state)
),
with_distance AS (

-- Step 2: Total sales per such country

           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_sales) AS rn,

WITH
-- Step 1: Sales with no promotion (LEFT JOIN on promotions yields NULL if no match)
no_promo_sales AS (
    SELECT s.*, t.calendar_quarter_desc, t.calendar_year
    FROM sales s
    JOIN times t ON s.time_id = t.time_id
    LEFT JOIN promotions p ON s.promo_id = p.promo_id
    WHERE p.promo_id IS NULL
),
-- US customers only, Q4 2019 and Q4 2020
us_sales AS (
    SELECT nps.*, c.cust_city, co.country_name
    FROM no_promo_sales nps
    JOIN customers c ON nps.cust_id = c.cust_id
    JOIN countries co ON c.country_id = co.country_id
    WHERE co.country_name = 'United States'
      AND nps.calendar_quarter_desc IN ('2019-04', '2020-04')
),
-- Aggregate sales by city and quarter
city_quarter_sales AS (
    SELECT cust_city, calendar_quarter_desc, SUM(amount_sold) AS total_sales
    FROM us_sales
    GROUP BY cust_city, calendar_quarter_desc
),
-- Pivot to have one row per city with both quarters
city_sales_pivot AS (
    SELECT 
        cust_city,
        MAX(CASE WHEN calendar_quarter_desc = '2019-04' THEN total_sales END) AS sales_2019,
        MAX(CASE WHEN calendar_quarter_desc = '2020-04' THEN total_sales END) AS sales_2020
    FROM city_quarter_sales
    GROUP BY cust_city
),
-- Keep only cities with at least 20% rise
qualified_cities AS (
    SELECT cust_city
    FROM city_sales_pivot
    WHERE sales_2019 IS NOT NULL AND sales_2020 IS NOT NULL
      AND (sales_2020 - sales_2019) / sales_2019 >= 0.20
),
-- All sales for those cities, both quarters
qualified_sales AS (
    SELECT us.*
    FROM us_sales us
    JOIN qualified_cities qc ON us.cust_city = qc.cust_city
),
-- Total sales per product across both quarters (for ranked selection)
product_total_sales AS (
    SELECT prod_id, SUM(amount_sold) AS total_sales
    FROM qualified_sales
    GROUP BY prod_id
),
-- Rank products by total sales, take top 20% using NTILE(5) (tile=1)
product_ranked AS (
    SELECT prod_id, total_sales,
           NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY total_sales DESC) AS tile
    FROM product_total_sales
),
top_products AS (
    SELECT prod_id
    FROM product_ranked
    WHERE tile = 1
),
-- For each top product, sales in each quarter
quarterly_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        prod_id,
        SUM(CASE WHEN calendar_quarter_desc = '2019-04' THEN amount_sold ELSE 0 END) AS sales_2019,
        SUM(CASE WHEN calendar_quarter_desc = '2020-04' THEN amount_sold ELSE 0 END) AS sales_2020
    FROM qualified_sales
    WHERE prod_id IN (SELECT prod_id FROM top_products)
    GROUP BY prod_id
),
-- Total sales per quarter for those cities
total_quarterly_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        calendar_quarter_desc,
        SUM(amount_sold) AS total_sales
    FROM qualified_sales
    WHERE calendar_quarter_desc IN ('2019-04','2020-04')
    GROUP BY calendar_quarter_desc
)
-- Final output: product, shares, and share change
SELECT 
    p.prod_name,
    qs.sales_2019 / t2019.total_sales AS share_2019,
    qs.sales_2020 / t2020.total_sales AS share_2020,
    (qs.sales_2020 / t2020.total_sales) - (qs.sales_2019 / t2019.total_sales) AS share_change
FROM quarterly_sales qs
JOIN top_products tp ON qs.prod_id = tp.prod_id
JOIN products p ON qs.prod_id = p.prod_id
CROSS JOIN (SELECT total_sales FROM total_quarterly_sales WHERE calendar_quarter_desc = '2019-04') t2019
CROSS JOIN (SELECT total_sales FROM total_quarterly_sales WHERE calendar_quarter_desc = '2020-04') t2020
ORDER BY share_change DESC

           CASE WHEN tq.total_2020 > 0 THEN ps.prod_sales_2020 * 1.0 / tq.total_2020 ELSE 0 END AS share_2020,
           ABS(CASE WHEN tq.total_2020 > 0 THEN ps.prod_sales_2020 * 1.0 / tq.total_2020 ELSE 0 END -

WITH
-- Filter sales to promotions with promo_total_id = 1 and channels with channel_total_id = 1
filtered_sales AS (
    SELECT
        s.amount_sold,
        s.time_id,
        s.prod_id,
        CAST(strftime('%Y', s.time_id) AS INTEGER) AS year,
        CAST(strftime('%m', s.time_id) AS INTEGER) AS month
    FROM sales s
    INNER JOIN promotions p ON s.promo_id = p.promo_id AND p.promo_total_id = 1
    INNER JOIN channels ch ON s.channel_id = ch.channel_id AND ch.channel_total_id = 1
),
-- Monthly sales per product in 2019
sales_2019 AS (
    SELECT prod_id, month, SUM(amount_sold) AS total_2019
    FROM filtered_sales
    WHERE year = 2019
    GROUP BY prod_id, month
),
-- Monthly sales per product in 2020
sales_2020 AS (
    SELECT prod_id, month, SUM(amount_sold) AS total_2020
    FROM filtered_sales
    WHERE year = 2020
    GROUP BY prod_id, month
),
-- Growth factor (2020 / 2019) for each product-month
growth AS (
    SELECT
        s19.prod_id,
        s19.month,
        s19.total_2019,
        s20.total_2020,
        (s20.total_2020 * 1.0 / s19.total_2019) AS growth_factor
    FROM sales_2019 s19
    INNER JOIN sales_2020 s20 ON s19.prod_id = s20.prod_id AND s19.month = s20.month
),
-- Projected 2021 monthly sales in local currency (France)
projected_local AS (
    SELECT
        g.prod_id,
        g.month,
        g.total_2020 * g.growth_factor AS projected_local_amount
    FROM growth g
),
-- Exchange rates from local currency (EUR) to USD for France in 2021
exchange_rates AS (
    SELECT month, to_us
    FROM currency
    WHERE year = 2021 AND country = 'France'
),
-- Convert projected amounts to USD
projected_usd AS (
    SELECT
        pl.month,
        pl.projected_local_amount * er.to_us AS projected_usd_amount
    FROM projected_local pl
    INNER JOIN exchange_rates er ON pl.month = er.month
),
-- Average projected USD sales per month
final AS (
    SELECT
        month,
        AVG(projected_usd_amount) AS avg_projected_usd
    FROM projected_usd
    GROUP BY month
)
-- List by month
SELECT month, avg_projected_usd
FROM final
ORDER BY month

WITH
-- Step 1: Monthly sales totals for 2019 and 2020, filtered by promo_total_id=1 and channel_total_id=1
monthly_sales AS (
    SELECT
        CAST(strftime('%Y', s.time_id) AS INTEGER) AS year,
        CAST(strftime('%m', s.time_id) AS INTEGER) AS month,
        SUM(s.amount_sold) AS total_sales
    FROM sales s
    JOIN promotions p ON s.promo_id = p.promo_id AND p.promo_total_id = 1
    JOIN channels c ON s.channel_id = c.channel_id AND c.channel_total_id = 1
    WHERE strftime('%Y', s.time_id) IN ('2019', '2020')
    GROUP BY year, month
),
-- Step 2: Separate 2019 and 2020 totals by month
sales_2019 AS (
    SELECT month, total_sales AS sales_2019
    FROM monthly_sales
    WHERE year = 2019
),
sales_2020 AS (
    SELECT month, total_sales AS sales_2020
    FROM monthly_sales
    WHERE year = 2020
),
-- Step 3: Compute growth rate (2020/2019) for each month where both exist and sales_2019 > 0
growth_rates AS (
    SELECT
        a.month,
        a.sales_2019,
        b.sales_2020,
        b.sales_2020 * 1.0 / a.sales_2019 AS growth_rate
    FROM sales_2019 a
    JOIN sales_2020 b ON a.month = b.month
    WHERE a.sales_2019 > 0
),
-- Step 4: Project 2021 monthly sales = 2020 sales * growth rate
projected_sales AS (
    SELECT
        g.month,
        g.sales_2020 * g.growth_rate AS projected_local
    FROM growth_rates g
),
-- Step 5: Convert projected sales to USD using France's exchange rate for 2021
converted_sales AS (
    SELECT
        p.month,
        p.projected_local * c.to_us AS projected_usd
    FROM projected_sales p
    JOIN currency c ON c.year = 2021 AND c.month = p.month AND c.country = 'France'
    WHERE c.to_us IS NOT NULL
),
-- Step 6: Compute median of the 12 monthly projected USD values
ordered_sales AS (
    SELECT
        projected_usd,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY projected_usd) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS cnt
    FROM converted_sales
)
SELECT
    AVG(projected_usd) AS median_projected_sales_usd
FROM ordered_sales
WHERE rn IN ((cnt + 1) / 2, (cnt + 2) / 2)

WITH italian_customers AS (
    SELECT cust.cust_id
    FROM customers cust
    JOIN countries c ON cust.country_id = c.country_id
    WHERE c.country_name = 'Italy'
),
dec2021_profit AS (
    SELECT s.cust_id,
           SUM(s.quantity_sold * (c.unit_price - c.unit_cost)) AS total_profit
    FROM sales s
    JOIN costs c ON s.prod_id = c.prod_id AND s.time_id = c.time_id
    JOIN times t ON s.time_id = t.time_id
    WHERE t.calendar_year = 2021
      AND t.calendar_month_name = 'December'
      AND s.cust_id IN (SELECT cust_id FROM italian_customers)
    GROUP BY s.cust_id
),
profit_range AS (
    SELECT MIN(total_profit) AS min_profit,
           MAX(total_profit) AS max_profit,
           (MAX(total_profit) - MIN(total_profit)) / 10.0 AS width
    FROM dec2021_profit
),
bucketed AS (
    SELECT p.cust_id,
           p.total_profit,
           CASE
               WHEN pr.width = 0 THEN 1
               ELSE 
                   CASE 
                       WHEN p.total_profit = pr.max_profit THEN 10
                       ELSE CAST( (p.total_profit - pr.min_profit) / pr.width AS INTEGER ) + 1
                   END
           END AS bucket_num
    FROM dec2021_profit p
    CROSS JOIN profit_range pr
),
bucket_series AS (
    SELECT 1 AS bucket_num
    UNION ALL
    SELECT bucket_num + 1
    FROM bucket_series
    WHERE bucket_num < 10
)
SELECT b.bucket_num,
       COALESCE(cnt.num_customers, 0) AS num_customers,
       cnt.min_profit,
       cnt.max_profit
FROM bucket_series b
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT bucket_num,
           COUNT(*) AS num_customers,
           MIN(total_profit) AS min_profit,
           MAX(total_profit) AS max_profit
    FROM bucketed
    GROUP BY bucket_num
) cnt ON b.bucket_num = cnt.bucket_num
ORDER BY b.bucket_num

customer_tiers AS (

WITH ranked_dates AS (
    -- Select one city per distinct date in July 2021 for Chinese cities
    SELECT 
        insert_date,
        city_name,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY insert_date ORDER BY population DESC, city_name) AS rn
    FROM cities
    WHERE country_code_2 = 'cn'
      AND insert_date >= '2021-07-01'
      AND insert_date < '2021-08-01'
),
one_per_date AS (
    SELECT insert_date, city_name
    FROM ranked_dates
    WHERE rn = 1
),
gap_flags AS (
    -- Identify where a new streak begins (difference > 1 day or NULL)
    SELECT 
        insert_date,
        city_name,
        CASE 
            WHEN julianday(insert_date) - julianday(LAG(insert_date) OVER (ORDER BY insert_date)) = 1 THEN 0
            ELSE 1
        END AS is_new_streak
    FROM one_per_date
),
streak_groups AS (
    -- Assign group IDs to consecutive dates
    SELECT 
        insert_date,
        city_name,
        SUM(is_new_streak) OVER (ORDER BY insert_date) AS streak_id
    FROM gap_flags
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Count length of each streak
    SELECT 
        streak_id,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_len
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY streak_id
),
min_max_streaks AS (
    -- Identify the shortest and longest streak lengths
    SELECT 
        MIN(streak_len) AS min_len,
        MAX(streak_len) AS max_len
    FROM streak_lengths
)
-- Final output: dates from the shortest and longest streaks, with capitalized city names
SELECT 
    sg.insert_date,
    UPPER(SUBSTR(sg.city_name, 1, 1)) || LOWER(SUBSTR(sg.city_name, 2)) AS city_name
FROM streak_groups sg
JOIN streak_lengths sl ON sg.streak_id = sl.streak_id
JOIN min_max_streaks mms ON sl.streak_len IN (mms.min_len, mms.max_len)
ORDER BY sg.insert_date

WITH june_cities AS (

),
ordered AS (

  FROM june_cities
),
groups AS (
  SELECT country_code_2, insert_date,
         DATE(insert_date, '-' || rn || ' days') AS grp_date
  FROM ordered
),
streaks AS (
  SELECT country_code_2, grp_date, COUNT(*) AS streak_len
  FROM groups
  GROUP BY country_code_2, grp_date

max_streaks AS (

WITH
-- Step 1: Find countries with exactly 9 distinct insertion days in January 2022
country_with_nine_days AS (
    SELECT country_code_2
    FROM cities
    WHERE insert_date LIKE '2022-01%'
    GROUP BY country_code_2
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),

-- Step 2: Get distinct insertion dates for that country in January 2022
distinct_dates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT insert_date AS dt
    FROM cities
    WHERE country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days LIMIT 1)
      AND insert_date LIKE '2022-01%'
),

-- Step 3: Assign a group number to each consecutive date sequence
-- Using the property: julianday(dt) - ROW_NUMBER() is constant for consecutive days
date_groups AS (
    SELECT dt,
           julianday(dt) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS grp
    FROM distinct_dates
),

-- Step 4: Compute each consecutive group's start, end, and length (days)
group_lengths AS (
    SELECT grp,
           MIN(dt) AS start_date,
           MAX(dt) AS end_date,
           COUNT(*) AS num_days
    FROM date_groups
    GROUP BY grp
),

-- Step 5: Select the longest consecutive period (break ties by earliest start)
longest_period AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM group_lengths
    ORDER BY num_days DESC, start_date ASC
    LIMIT 1
),

-- Step 6: All city entries for that country that fall within the longest consecutive period
entries_in_period AS (
    SELECT capital
    FROM cities
    WHERE country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days LIMIT 1)
      AND insert_date >= (SELECT start_date FROM longest_period)
      AND insert_date <= (SELECT end_date FROM longest_period)
)

-- Step 7: Calculate proportion of capital entries (capital = 1)
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN capital = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS proportion_capital
FROM entries_in_period

WITH years(year) AS (
  VALUES (2021), (2022), (2023)
),
months(month_num) AS (
  VALUES ('04'), ('05'), ('06')
),
year_month AS (
  SELECT y.year, m.month_num
  FROM years y
  CROSS JOIN months m
),
city_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    CAST(strftime('%Y', insert_date) AS INTEGER) AS year,
    strftime('%m', insert_date) AS month_num,
    COUNT(*) AS city_count
  FROM cities
  WHERE strftime('%Y', insert_date) IN ('2021', '2022', '2023')
    AND strftime('%m', insert_date) IN ('04', '05', '06')
  GROUP BY year, month_num
),
all_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    ym.year,
    ym.month_num,
    COALESCE(cc.city_count, 0) AS city_count
  FROM year_month ym
  LEFT JOIN city_counts cc ON ym.year = cc.year AND ym.month_num = cc.month_num
),
running_total_cte AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    month_num,
    city_count,
    SUM(city_count) OVER (
      PARTITION BY month_num 
      ORDER BY year 
      ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
    ) AS running_total
  FROM all_counts
),
with_lag AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    month_num,
    city_count,
    running_total,
    LAG(city_count) OVER (PARTITION BY month_num ORDER BY year) AS prev_city_count,
    LAG(running_total) OVER (PARTITION BY month_num ORDER BY year) AS prev_running_total
  FROM running_total_cte
)
SELECT 
  year,
  CASE month_num
    WHEN '04' THEN 'April'
    WHEN '05' THEN 'May'
    WHEN '06' THEN 'June'
  END AS month,
  city_count AS total_cities_added,
  running_total AS cumulative_running_total,
  ROUND(
    (city_count - prev_city_count) * 100.0 / NULLIF(prev_city_count, 0), 
    2
  ) AS monthly_yoy_growth_percent,
  ROUND(
    (running_total - prev_running_total) * 100.0 / NULLIF(prev_running_total, 0), 
    2
  ) AS running_total_yoy_growth_percent
FROM with_lag
WHERE year IN (2022, 2023)
ORDER BY year, month_num

WITH step1 AS (

    pn.pizza_name,

    pco.extras,
    pr.toppings AS std_toppings,
    pco.order_time
  FROM pizza_customer_orders pco
  JOIN pizza_names pn ON pco.pizza_id = pn.pizza_id
  JOIN pizza_recipes pr ON pco.pizza_id = pr.pizza_id
),
std_toppings_split AS (
  SELECT row_id, TRIM(value) AS topping_id
  FROM step1, JSON_EACH('["' || REPLACE(std_toppings, ',', '","') || '"]')

),
exclusions_split AS (
  SELECT row_id, TRIM(value) AS topping_id
  FROM step1, JSON_EACH('["' || REPLACE(COALESCE(exclusions, ''), ',', '","') || '"]')
  WHERE value != ''
),
extras_split AS (
  SELECT row_id, TRIM(value) AS topping_id
  FROM step1, JSON_EACH('["' || REPLACE(COALESCE(extras, ''), ',', '","') || '"]')
  WHERE value != ''

  FROM (
    SELECT st.row_id, st.topping_id

      SELECT 1 FROM exclusions_split es
      WHERE es.row_id = st.row_id AND es.topping_id = st.topping_id

    SELECT ex.row_id, ex.topping_id
    FROM extras_split ex
  )
  GROUP BY row_id, topping_id
),
final_details AS (
  SELECT
    ft.row_id,
    ft.topping_id,
    ft.cnt,

)

  s.order_id,

  s.pizza_name,
  s.pizza_name || ': ' ||

      CASE WHEN fd.cnt > 1 THEN fd.cnt || 'x ' || fd.topping_name ELSE fd.topping_name END,
      ', '
      ORDER BY (CASE WHEN fd.cnt > 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END), fd.topping_name
    )
    FROM final_details fd
    WHERE fd.row_id = s.row_id
    GROUP BY fd.row_id),
    ''

FROM step1 s

WITH RECURSIVE

    SELECT 
        pr.pizza_id, 
        CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(pr.toppings, 1, INSTR(pr.toppings || ',', ',') - 1)) AS INTEGER) AS topping_id,
        SUBSTR(pr.toppings, INSTR(pr.toppings || ',', ',') + 1) AS rest
    FROM pizza_recipes pr
    WHERE pr.toppings IS NOT NULL AND pr.toppings != ''

    SELECT 
        rt.pizza_id,
        CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(rt.rest, 1, INSTR(rt.rest || ',', ',') - 1)) AS INTEGER),
        SUBSTR(rt.rest, INSTR(rt.rest || ',', ',') + 1)
    FROM recipe_toppings rt
    WHERE rt.rest IS NOT NULL AND rt.rest != ''
),
exclusions AS (
    SELECT 
        pco.order_id,
        pco.pizza_id,
        CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(pco.exclusions, 1, INSTR(pco.exclusions || ',', ',') - 1)) AS INTEGER) AS exclusion_topping,
        SUBSTR(pco.exclusions, INSTR(pco.exclusions || ',', ',') + 1) AS rest
    FROM pizza_clean_customer_orders pco
    WHERE pco.exclusions IS NOT NULL AND pco.exclusions != ''
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        e.order_id,
        e.pizza_id,
        CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(e.rest, 1, INSTR(e.rest || ',', ',') - 1)) AS INTEGER),
        SUBSTR(e.rest, INSTR(e.rest || ',', ',') + 1)
    FROM exclusions e
    WHERE e.rest IS NOT NULL AND e.rest != ''

    SELECT

        SUBSTR(pco.extras, INSTR(pco.extras || ',', ',') + 1) AS rest
    FROM pizza_clean_customer_orders pco
    WHERE pco.extras IS NOT NULL AND pco.extras != ''
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        x.order_id,
        x.pizza_id,
        CAST(TRIM(SUBSTR(x.rest, 1, INSTR(x.rest || ',', ',') - 1)) AS INTEGER),
        SUBSTR(x.rest, INSTR(x.rest || ',', ',') + 1)
    FROM extras x
    WHERE x.rest IS NOT NULL AND x.rest != ''
),
base_non_excluded AS (
    SELECT 
        pco.order_id,
        pco.pizza_id,
        rt.topping_id
    FROM pizza_clean_customer_orders pco

    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM exclusions e 
        WHERE e.order_id = pco.order_id

          AND e.exclusion_topping = rt.topping_id
    )
),
extras_added AS (
    SELECT 
        e.order_id,
        e.pizza_id,

delivered_orders AS (

FROM (
    SELECT order_id, pizza_id, topping_id FROM base_non_excluded

    SELECT order_id, pizza_id, topping_id FROM extras_added
) all_toppings
JOIN pizza_toppings pt ON all_toppings.topping_id = pt.topping_id

GROUP BY pt.topping_name
ORDER BY pt.topping_name

WITH non_cancelled_orders AS (

        co.pizza_id,
        pn.pizza_name,

            WHEN pn.pizza_name = 'Meatlovers' THEN 12

WITH monthly_net AS (

    FROM customer_transactions

        MAX(month) AS max_month

        customer_id,

filled_months AS (

WITH 
-- Step 1: Compute net change per user per month (deposits - withdrawals - purchases)
user_monthly_net AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    strftime('%Y-%m-01', txn_date) AS month_start,
    SUM(CASE WHEN txn_type = 'deposit' THEN txn_amount ELSE -txn_amount END) AS net_change
  FROM customer_transactions
  GROUP BY customer_id, month_start
),

-- Step 2: Compute cumulative balance per user at the end of each month
user_cumulative AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    month_start,
    SUM(net_change) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month_start) AS balance
  FROM user_monthly_net
),

-- Step 3: Replace negative balances with zero and sum across all users per month
monthly_total_balance AS (
  SELECT 
    month_start,
    SUM(CASE WHEN balance < 0 THEN 0 ELSE balance END) AS total_balance
  FROM user_cumulative
  GROUP BY month_start
),

-- Step 4: Get previous month's total balance using LAG
monthly_with_previous AS (
  SELECT 
    month_start,
    total_balance,
    LAG(total_balance) OVER (ORDER BY month_start) AS previous_total_balance
  FROM monthly_total_balance
)

-- Final: Output previous month's total balance, exclude first month
SELECT 
  month_start AS month,
  previous_total_balance AS total_balance_previous_month
FROM monthly_with_previous
WHERE previous_total_balance IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY month_start

WITH
-- Step 1: Daily net transaction amounts per customer
daily_net AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        txn_date,
        SUM(
            CASE WHEN txn_type = 'deposit' THEN txn_amount ELSE -txn_amount END
        ) AS net_amount
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id, txn_date
),

-- Step 2: Range of dates for each customer
customer_dates AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        MIN(txn_date) AS min_date,
        MAX(txn_date) AS max_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
),

-- Step 3: Generate all calendar dates between min and max for each customer
date_series AS (
    SELECT customer_id, min_date AS date
    FROM customer_dates
    UNION ALL
    SELECT ds.customer_id, DATE(ds.date, '+1 day')
    FROM date_series ds
    JOIN customer_dates cd ON ds.customer_id = cd.customer_id
    WHERE ds.date < cd.max_date
),

-- Step 4: Daily running balance
daily_balance AS (
    SELECT
        ds.customer_id,
        ds.date,
        COALESCE(dn.net_amount, 0) AS net_today,
        SUM(COALESCE(dn.net_amount, 0)) OVER (
            PARTITION BY ds.customer_id
            ORDER BY ds.date
            ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
        ) AS running_balance
    FROM date_series ds
    LEFT JOIN daily_net dn ON ds.customer_id = dn.customer_id AND ds.date = dn.txn_date
),

-- Step 5: 30-day rolling average balance (only after 30 days of data)
rolling_avg AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        date,
        AVG(running_balance) OVER (
            PARTITION BY customer_id
            ORDER BY date
            ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS avg_30,
        COUNT(*) OVER (
            PARTITION BY customer_id
            ORDER BY date
            ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW

    FROM daily_balance
),

-- Step 6: Treat negative averages as zero, ignore days with < 30 days history
daily_rolling_avg AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        date,
        CASE
            WHEN cnt >= 30 THEN
                CASE WHEN avg_30 < 0 THEN 0 ELSE avg_30 END
            ELSE NULL
        END AS rolling_avg_30
    FROM rolling_avg
),

-- Step 7: Monthly maximum of the rolling averages per customer
monthly_max AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        strftime('%Y-%m', date) AS month,
        MAX(rolling_avg_30) AS max_avg
    FROM daily_rolling_avg
    WHERE rolling_avg_30 IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY customer_id, month
),

-- Step 8: Identify each customer's first month
first_month AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(txn_date)) AS first_month
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
)

-- Step 9: Exclude first month and sum across customers

    m.month,
    SUM(m.max_avg) AS total_max_avg
FROM monthly_max m

    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
-- Step 3: Generate every date between min and max for each customer using recursive CTE
date_series AS (
    SELECT customer_id, min_date AS date, max_date
    FROM customer_date_range
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        d.customer_id, 
        DATE(d.date, '+1 day') AS date,
        d.max_date
    FROM date_series d
    WHERE DATE(d.date, '+1 day') <= d.max_date
),
-- Step 4: Attach daily net (0 if no transaction) to the date series
daily_with_net AS (
    SELECT 
        ds.customer_id, 
        ds.date, 
        COALESCE(dn.net, 0) AS net
    FROM date_series ds
    LEFT JOIN daily_net dn 
        ON ds.customer_id = dn.customer_id AND ds.date = dn.txn_date
),
-- Step 5: Compute daily balance recursively, applying floor at zero
balance_cte AS (
    -- Anchor: first day for each customer (previous balance = 0)
    SELECT 
        customer_id, 
        date, 
        net, 
        CASE WHEN net < 0 THEN 0 ELSE net END AS balance
    FROM daily_with_net
    WHERE date = (SELECT min_date FROM customer_date_range cd WHERE cd.customer_id = daily_with_net.customer_id)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive step: balance = max(0, previous_balance + current net)
    SELECT 
        dw.customer_id, 
        dw.date, 
        dw.net,
        CASE WHEN bc.balance + dw.net < 0 THEN 0 ELSE bc.balance + dw.net END AS balance
    FROM balance_cte bc
    JOIN daily_with_net dw 
        ON bc.customer_id = dw.customer_id 
        AND DATE(dw.date, '-1 day') = bc.date
)
-- Step 6: For each customer-month, get the maximum daily balance
, monthly_max AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        SUBSTR(date, 1, 7) AS month,
        MAX(balance) AS max_balance
    FROM balance_cte
    GROUP BY customer_id, month
)
-- Step 7: Sum the maximums across customers per month
SELECT 
    month,
    SUM(max_balance) AS total_max_balance
FROM monthly_max
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month

WITH
-- Step 1: Identify customers who placed orders in 1998
customers_1998 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT customerid
    FROM orders
    WHERE strftime('%Y', orderdate) = '1998'
),
-- Step 2: Compute total spending per customer (excluding discounts)
customer_totals AS (
    SELECT o.customerid, SUM(od.unitprice * od.quantity) AS total_spent
    FROM orders o
    INNER JOIN order_details od ON o.orderid = od.orderid
    WHERE strftime('%Y', o.orderdate) = '1998'
    GROUP BY o.customerid
),
-- Step 3: Count total customers in 1998 for percentage calculation
total_customers AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM customer_totals
),
-- Step 4: Assign each customer to a spending group using the thresholds
customer_groups AS (
    SELECT ct.customerid, ct.total_spent, cgt.groupname
    FROM customer_totals ct
    LEFT JOIN customergroupthreshold cgt
        ON ct.total_spent >= cgt.rangebottom AND ct.total_spent <= cgt.rangetop
)
-- Step 5: Aggregate by group and compute percentage
SELECT
    cg.groupname,
    COUNT(*) AS customer_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / tc.cnt, 2) AS percentage
FROM customer_groups cg
CROSS JOIN total_customers tc
GROUP BY cg.groupname
ORDER BY customer_count DESC

WITH english_grades AS (
    SELECT ss.StudentID, ss.Grade
    FROM Student_Schedules ss
    JOIN Classes c ON ss.ClassID = c.ClassID
    JOIN Subjects sub ON c.SubjectID = sub.SubjectID
    WHERE sub.SubjectCode LIKE 'ENG%' 
        AND ss.ClassStatus = 2
),
total AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_rows FROM english_grades
),
grade_counts AS (
    SELECT Grade, COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM english_grades
    GROUP BY Grade
),
cumulative AS (
    SELECT Grade, cnt,
           SUM(cnt) OVER (ORDER BY Grade DESC) AS cum_cnt
    FROM grade_counts
)
SELECT 
    st.StudLastName,
    CASE 
        WHEN cum.cum_cnt * 1.0 / t.total_rows <= 0.2 THEN 'First'
        WHEN cum.cum_cnt * 1.0 / t.total_rows <= 0.4 THEN 'Second'
        WHEN cum.cum_cnt * 1.0 / t.total_rows <= 0.6 THEN 'Third'
        WHEN cum.cum_cnt * 1.0 / t.total_rows <= 0.8 THEN 'Fourth'
        ELSE 'Fifth'
    END AS quintile
FROM english_grades eg
JOIN cumulative cum ON eg.Grade = cum.Grade
CROSS JOIN total t
JOIN Students st ON eg.StudentID = st.StudentID
ORDER BY cum.cum_cnt * 1.0 / t.total_rows ASC

WITH 
-- Step 1: Movies with average rating above 8
rated_above_8 AS (
    SELECT movie_id
    FROM ratings
    WHERE avg_rating > 8
),

-- Step 2: Top 3 genres with the most movies rated above 8
genre_counts AS (
    SELECT g.genre, COUNT(DISTINCT g.movie_id) AS movie_count
    FROM genre g
    INNER JOIN rated_above_8 r ON g.movie_id = r.movie_id
    GROUP BY g.genre
    ORDER BY movie_count DESC
    LIMIT 3
),

-- Step 3: Movies that belong to any of the top genres and are rated above 8
top_genre_movies AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT g.movie_id
    FROM genre g
    INNER JOIN rated_above_8 r ON g.movie_id = r.movie_id
    INNER JOIN genre_counts gc ON g.genre = gc.genre
),

-- Step 4: Count movies directed per director for those movies, get top 4
director_counts AS (
    SELECT dm.name_id, COUNT(DISTINCT dm.movie_id) AS movie_count
    FROM director_mapping dm
    INNER JOIN top_genre_movies t ON dm.movie_id = t.movie_id
    GROUP BY dm.name_id
    ORDER BY movie_count DESC, dm.name_id
    LIMIT 4
)

-- Step 5: Retrieve director names
SELECT n.name AS director_name, dc.movie_count
FROM director_counts dc
INNER JOIN names n ON dc.name_id = n.id
ORDER BY dc.movie_count DESC, n.name
pred.local156:
WITH transaction_spend AS (
  -- Calculate dollar amount spent per transaction for Bitcoin purchases
  SELECT
    bt.member_id,
    bt.quantity,
    bp.price,
    bt.txn_date,
    CAST(SUBSTR(bt.txn_date, 7, 4) AS INTEGER) AS year,
    bt.quantity * bp.price AS spent
  FROM bitcoin_transactions bt
  JOIN bitcoin_prices bp
    ON bt.ticker = bp.ticker
    AND bt.txn_date = bp.market_date
  WHERE bt.ticker = 'BTC'
    AND bt.txn_type = 'BUY'
),
region_year_totals AS (
  -- Aggregate total spent and total quantity per region per year
  SELECT
    bm.region,
    ts.year,
    SUM(ts.spent) AS total_spent,
    SUM(ts.quantity) AS total_quantity
  FROM transaction_spend ts
  JOIN bitcoin_members bm ON ts.member_id = bm.member_id
  GROUP BY bm.region, ts.year
),
region_first_year AS (
  -- Identify the first purchase year for each region (to exclude later)
  SELECT
    region,
    MIN(year) AS first_year
  FROM region_year_totals
  GROUP BY region
),
filtered_yearly_avg AS (
  -- Exclude first year for each region and compute average purchase price
  SELECT
    ryt.region,
    ryt.year,
    ryt.total_spent / ryt.total_quantity AS avg_price
  FROM region_year_totals ryt
  JOIN region_first_year rfy
    ON ryt.region = rfy.region
    AND ryt.year > rfy.first_year
),
ranked AS (
  -- Rank regions by average price descending per year
  SELECT
    region,
    year,
    avg_price,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY avg_price DESC) AS rank
  FROM filtered_yearly_avg
),
with_pct_change AS (
  -- Calculate year-over-year percentage change for each region
  SELECT
    region,
    year,
    avg_price,
    rank,
    (avg_price - LAG(avg_price) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY year)) 
    / LAG(avg_price) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY year) * 100 AS pct_change
  FROM ranked
)
-- Final output ordered by year and region
SELECT
  year,
  region,
  ROUND(avg_price, 2) AS avg_purchase_price,
  rank,
  ROUND(pct_change, 2) AS annual_pct_change
FROM with_pct_change
ORDER BY year, region

WITH volume_conversion AS (
  SELECT
    ticker,

    volume,

      SUBSTR(market_date, 7, 4) || '-' ||

  SELECT

    market_date,

pred.local170:
WITH 
-- Step 1: Identify each legislator's first term start and the state from that term
first_term_rn AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id_bioguide ORDER BY term_start) AS rn
    FROM legislators_terms
),
first_terms AS (
    SELECT id_bioguide, term_start AS first_start, state
    FROM first_term_rn
    WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Step 2: Join with legislators to get gender and build the cohort of legislators with a first term
cohort AS (
    SELECT ft.id_bioguide,
           ft.first_start,
           ft.state,
           lg.gender
    FROM first_terms ft
    JOIN legislators lg ON ft.id_bioguide = lg.id_bioguide
),
-- Step 3: Keep only states that have at least one male AND one female legislator
state_gender_counts AS (
    SELECT state, gender, COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM cohort
    GROUP BY state, gender
),
eligible_states AS (
    SELECT state
    FROM state_gender_counts
    GROUP BY state
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT gender) = 2 AND MIN(cnt) > 0
),
cohort_eligible AS (
    SELECT c.*
    FROM cohort c
    WHERE c.state IN (SELECT state FROM eligible_states)
),
-- Step 4: Define the six time offsets
offsets(off) AS (
    VALUES (0), (2), (4), (6), (8), (10)
),
-- Step 5: Build all (legislator, offset) pairs and compute the check date (Dec 31 of the year)
check_dates AS (
    SELECT ce.id_bioguide,
           ce.state,
           ce.gender,
           o.off,
           printf('%d-12-31',
                  CAST(strftime('%Y', ce.first_start) AS INTEGER) + o.off
           ) AS check_date
    FROM cohort_eligible ce
    CROSS JOIN offsets o
),
-- Step 6: For each (state, gender, offset) count how many distinct legislators have a term covering that check date
retention_raw AS (
    SELECT cd.state,
           cd.gender,
           cd.off,
           COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN lt.id_bioguide IS NOT NULL THEN cd.id_bioguide END) AS retained_count
    FROM check_dates cd
    LEFT JOIN legislators_terms lt
        ON cd.id_bioguide = lt.id_bioguide
        AND lt.term_start <= cd.check_date
        AND lt.term_end >= cd.check_date
    GROUP BY cd.state, cd.gender, cd.off
),
-- Step 7: For each state and gender, count number of offsets where retention > 0
state_gender_ok AS (
    SELECT state, gender,
           SUM(CASE WHEN retained_count > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS offsets_ok
    FROM retention_raw
    GROUP BY state, gender
)
-- Step 8: Keep states where both genders have all 6 offsets with retention > 0
SELECT state
FROM state_gender_ok
GROUP BY state
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT gender) = 2 AND MIN(offsets_ok) = 6
ORDER BY state

WITH first_pay AS (
    SELECT customer_id, MIN(payment_date) AS first_date
    FROM payment
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
customer_ltv AS (
    SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) AS total_ltv
    FROM payment
    GROUP BY customer_id
    HAVING total_ltv > 0
),
payment_with_diff AS (
    SELECT p.customer_id, p.amount, p.payment_date, fp.first_date,
           (julianday(p.payment_date) - julianday(fp.first_date)) AS day_diff
    FROM payment p
    INNER JOIN first_pay fp ON p.customer_id = fp.customer_id
    WHERE p.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM customer_ltv)
),
customer_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        pwd.customer_id,
        MAX(cl.total_ltv) AS total_ltv,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN pwd.day_diff <= 7.0 THEN pwd.amount ELSE 0 END), 0) AS sum_7,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN pwd.day_diff <= 30.0 THEN pwd.amount ELSE 0 END), 0) AS sum_30
    FROM payment_with_diff pwd
    INNER JOIN customer_ltv cl ON pwd.customer_id = cl.customer_id
    GROUP BY pwd.customer_id
)
SELECT 
    AVG(total_ltv) AS avg_total_ltv,
    AVG(sum_7 * 100.0 / total_ltv) AS avg_7day_pct,
    AVG(sum_30 * 100.0 / total_ltv) AS avg_30day_pct
FROM customer_summary

    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customer_id 
      ORDER BY payment_date, rental_id
    ) AS rn
  FROM payment
),
first_rental AS (
  -- Keep only the first payment (and thus first rental)
  SELECT customer_id, rental_id
  FROM first_payment
  WHERE rn = 1
),
first_movie_rating AS (
  -- Get the rating of the movie rented in that first transaction
  SELECT 
    fr.customer_id,
    f.rating
  FROM first_rental fr
  JOIN rental r ON fr.rental_id = r.rental_id
  JOIN inventory i ON r.inventory_id = i.inventory_id
  JOIN film f ON i.film_id = f.film_id
),
customer_amount AS (
  -- Total amount spent per customer (all payments)
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM payment
  GROUP BY customer_id
),
customer_rentals AS (
  -- Total number of rentals per customer (all rental records)

    COUNT(*) AS num_rentals
  FROM rental
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT 
  fmr.rating,
  AVG(ca.total_amount) AS avg_total_amount,

WITH top10_customers AS (
    -- Step 1: Identify top 10 paying customers by total payment amount
    SELECT customer_id
    FROM payment
    GROUP BY customer_id
    ORDER BY SUM(amount) DESC
    LIMIT 10
),
monthly_totals AS (
    -- Step 2: For those customers, compute monthly payment totals
    SELECT p.customer_id,
           strftime('%Y-%m', p.payment_date) AS month,
           SUM(p.amount) AS monthly_total
    FROM payment p
    JOIN top10_customers t ON p.customer_id = t.customer_id
    GROUP BY p.customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', p.payment_date)
),
with_previous AS (
    -- Step 3: Add previous month's total for each customer
    SELECT customer_id,
           month,
           monthly_total,
           LAG(monthly_total) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_monthly_total,
           monthly_total - LAG(monthly_total) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS diff
    FROM monthly_totals
),
ranked AS (
    -- Step 4: Rank by absolute month-over-month difference
    SELECT customer_id,

    FROM with_previous
    WHERE diff IS NOT NULL
)
-- Step 5: Output customer details, month, and rounded difference

       c.first_name || ' ' || c.last_name AS customer_name,
       r.month,
       ROUND(r.diff, 2) AS month_over_month_difference
FROM ranked r
JOIN customer c ON r.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE r.rn = 1

WITH RECURSIVE 
distinct_words AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT words AS word FROM word_list
),
chars(word, ch, idx) AS (
    SELECT word, SUBSTR(word, 1, 1), 1 FROM distinct_words
    UNION ALL
    SELECT word, SUBSTR(word, idx+1, 1), idx+1 FROM chars WHERE idx < LENGTH(word)
),
word_sorted AS (
    SELECT word, GROUP_CONCAT(ch, '' ORDER BY ch) AS sorted_letters
    FROM chars
    GROUP BY word
),
anagram_groups AS (
    SELECT sorted_letters, COUNT(*) AS group_count
    FROM word_sorted
    GROUP BY sorted_letters
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
),
candidate_words AS (
    SELECT w.word, ag.group_count - 1 AS anagram_count
    FROM distinct_words w
    JOIN word_sorted ws ON w.word = ws.word
    JOIN anagram_groups ag ON ws.sorted_letters = ag.sorted_letters
    WHERE LENGTH(w.word) IN (4, 5)
      AND SUBSTR(w.word, 1, 1) = 'r'
)
SELECT word, anagram_count
FROM candidate_words
ORDER BY word ASC
LIMIT 10

WITH
    -- Extract goals for home and away teams per match
    home_goals AS (
        SELECT
            home_team_api_id AS team_id,
            season,
            home_team_goal AS goal
        FROM Match
    ),
    away_goals AS (
        SELECT
            away_team_api_id AS team_id,
            season,
            away_team_goal AS goal
        FROM Match
    ),
    -- Combine home and away goals
    all_goals AS (
        SELECT * FROM home_goals
        UNION ALL
        SELECT * FROM away_goals
    ),
    -- Total goals per team per season
    team_season_total AS (
        SELECT
            team_id,
            season,
            SUM(goal) AS total_goals
        FROM all_goals
        GROUP BY team_id, season
    ),
    -- Maximum total goals per team across seasons
    team_max_goals AS (
        SELECT
            team_id,
            MAX(total_goals) AS max_goals
        FROM team_season_total
        GROUP BY team_id
    ),
    -- Rank the max goals and count total rows
    ranked AS (
        SELECT
            max_goals,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY max_goals) AS rn,
            COUNT(*) OVER () AS cnt
        FROM team_max_goals
    )
-- Compute the median (average of middle two if even count)
SELECT AVG(max_goals) AS median
FROM ranked
WHERE rn IN ((cnt + 1) / 2, (cnt + 2) / 2)

WITH 
-- All distinct teams per league
team_league AS (
    SELECT league_id, home_team_api_id AS team_api_id
    FROM Match
    UNION
    SELECT league_id, away_team_api_id
    FROM Match
),
-- Wins: only matches with a clear winner
wins AS (
    SELECT 
        league_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN home_team_goal > away_team_goal THEN home_team_api_id
            WHEN away_team_goal > home_team_goal THEN away_team_api_id
        END AS winner_team_api_id
    FROM Match
    WHERE home_team_goal != away_team_goal
),
-- Count wins per team per league (including zero)
win_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        tl.league_id,
        tl.team_api_id,
        COUNT(w.winner_team_api_id) AS win_count
    FROM team_league tl
    LEFT JOIN wins w 
        ON tl.league_id = w.league_id AND tl.team_api_id = w.winner_team_api_id
    GROUP BY tl.league_id, tl.team_api_id
),
-- Rank teams within each league by fewest wins (tie‑break by team name)
ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        l.name AS league_name,
        t.team_long_name,
        wc.win_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY wc.league_id 
            ORDER BY wc.win_count ASC, t.team_long_name ASC
        ) AS rn
    FROM win_counts wc
    JOIN League l ON wc.league_id = l.id
    JOIN Team t ON wc.team_api_id = t.team_api_id
)
-- Final output: one team per league with fewest wins
SELECT league_name, team_long_name, win_count
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY league_name

WITH bat_runs AS (

    JOIN ball_by_ball b
        ON bs.match_id = b.match_id
        AND bs.innings_no = b.innings_no
        AND bs.over_id = b.over_id
        AND bs.ball_id = b.ball_id

        ON bs.match_id = m.match_id
    GROUP BY m.season_id, b.striker
),
ranked_bat AS (
    SELECT season_id,
           player_id,
           total_runs,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
               PARTITION BY season_id
               ORDER BY total_runs DESC, player_id ASC
           ) AS rnk
    FROM bat_runs
),
top_bat AS (
    SELECT season_id, player_id, total_runs, rnk
    FROM ranked_bat
    WHERE rnk <= 3
),
bowler_wickets AS (
    SELECT m.season_id,
           b.bowler AS player_id,
           COUNT(*) AS wickets
    FROM wicket_taken w

        ON w.match_id = b.match_id
        AND w.innings_no = b.innings_no
        AND w.over_id = b.over_id
        AND w.ball_id = b.ball_id

        ON w.match_id = m.match_id

    GROUP BY m.season_id, b.bowler
),
ranked_bowl AS (
    SELECT season_id,
           player_id,
           wickets,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
               PARTITION BY season_id
               ORDER BY wickets DESC, player_id ASC
           ) AS rnk
    FROM bowler_wickets
),
top_bowl AS (
    SELECT season_id, player_id, wickets, rnk
    FROM ranked_bowl
    WHERE rnk <= 3
)

       p_bowl.player_name AS bowler,
       tbw.wickets
FROM top_bat tb

WITH ball_with_pair AS (
    SELECT 
        b.match_id,
        b.over_id,
        b.ball_id,
        b.innings_no,
        b.striker,
        b.non_striker,
        CASE WHEN b.striker < b.non_striker THEN b.striker ELSE b.non_striker END AS p_low,
        CASE WHEN b.striker > b.non_striker THEN b.striker ELSE b.non_striker END AS p_high,
        IFNULL(r.runs_scored, 0) AS runs_scored
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    LEFT JOIN batsman_scored r 
        ON b.match_id = r.match_id 
        AND b.over_id = r.over_id 
        AND b.ball_id = r.ball_id 
        AND b.innings_no = r.innings_no
),
partnership_runs AS (
    SELECT 
        match_id,
        p_low,
        p_high,
        SUM(CASE WHEN striker = p_low THEN runs_scored ELSE 0 END) AS runs_low,
        SUM(CASE WHEN striker = p_high THEN runs_scored ELSE 0 END) AS runs_high,
        SUM(runs_scored) AS total_runs
    FROM ball_with_pair
    GROUP BY match_id, p_low, p_high
),
ranked_partnerships AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY match_id ORDER BY total_runs DESC) AS rnk
    FROM partnership_runs
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN runs_low > runs_high THEN p_low
        WHEN runs_low < runs_high THEN p_high
        ELSE p_high
    END AS player1_id,
    CASE 
        WHEN runs_low > runs_high THEN runs_low
        WHEN runs_low < runs_high THEN runs_high
        ELSE runs_high
    END AS player1_score,
    CASE 
        WHEN runs_low > runs_high THEN p_high
        WHEN runs_low < runs_high THEN p_low
        ELSE p_low
    END AS player2_id,
    CASE 
        WHEN runs_low > runs_high THEN runs_high
        WHEN runs_low < runs_high THEN runs_low
        ELSE runs_low
    END AS player2_score,
    total_runs AS partnership_total
FROM ranked_partnerships
WHERE rnk = 1
ORDER BY match_id, player1_id

WITH illegal_balls AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id
    FROM extra_runs
    WHERE extra_type IN ('wides','noballs')
),
ball_legal AS (
    SELECT b.match_id, b.innings_no, b.over_id, b.ball_id, b.bowler,
           CASE WHEN ib.match_id IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_legal
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    LEFT JOIN illegal_balls ib
        ON b.match_id = ib.match_id
        AND b.innings_no = ib.innings_no
        AND b.over_id = ib.over_id
        AND b.ball_id = ib.ball_id
),
delivery_with_runs AS (
    SELECT bl.match_id, bl.innings_no, bl.over_id, bl.ball_id, bl.bowler, bl.is_legal,
           COALESCE(bs.runs_scored, 0) AS runs_off_bat
    FROM ball_legal bl
    LEFT JOIN batsman_scored bs
        ON bl.match_id = bs.match_id
        AND bl.innings_no = bs.innings_no
        AND bl.over_id = bs.over_id
        AND bl.ball_id = bs.ball_id
),
delivery_with_wickets AS (
    SELECT dr.*,
           CASE WHEN wt.kind_out IS NOT NULL AND wt.kind_out IN ('bowled','caught','lbw','stumped','hit wicket')
                THEN 1 ELSE 0
           END AS wicket_flag
    FROM delivery_with_runs dr
    LEFT JOIN wicket_taken wt
        ON dr.match_id = wt.match_id
        AND dr.innings_no = wt.innings_no
        AND dr.over_id = wt.over_id
        AND dr.ball_id = wt.ball_id
),
per_bowler AS (
    SELECT bowler,
           SUM(runs_off_bat) AS total_runs,
           SUM(is_legal) AS total_legal_balls,
           COUNT(*) AS total_balls,
           SUM(wicket_flag) AS total_wickets,
           ROUND(SUM(runs_off_bat) * 6.0 / NULLIF(SUM(is_legal), 0), 2) AS economy_rate,
           ROUND(COUNT(*) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(wicket_flag), 0), 2) AS strike_rate
    FROM delivery_with_wickets
    GROUP BY bowler
),
per_match_bowler AS (
    SELECT bowler, match_id,
           SUM(runs_off_bat) AS match_runs,
           SUM(wicket_flag) AS match_wickets
    FROM delivery_with_wickets
    GROUP BY bowler, match_id
),
best_bowling AS (
    SELECT bowler,
           match_wickets || '-' || match_runs AS best_performance
    FROM (
        SELECT bowler, match_id, match_wickets, match_runs,
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY bowler ORDER BY match_wickets DESC, match_runs ASC) AS rn
        FROM per_match_bowler
    ) t
    WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT p.player_name,
       pb.total_wickets,
       pb.economy_rate,
       pb.strike_rate,
       bb.best_performance
FROM per_bowler pb
JOIN best_bowling bb ON pb.bowler = bb.bowler
JOIN player p ON pb.bowler = p.player_id
ORDER BY p.player_name

WITH
  -- Player roles frequency
  role_counts AS (
    SELECT player_id, role, COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM player_match
    GROUP BY player_id, role
  ),
  ranked_roles AS (
    SELECT player_id, role,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS rn
    FROM role_counts
  ),
  -- Per match batting runs
  batsman_match_runs AS (
    SELECT b.striker AS player_id, b.match_id,
           SUM(bs.runs_scored) AS runs
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    JOIN batsman_scored bs USING(match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id)
    GROUP BY b.striker, b.match_id
  ),
  -- Player batting summary
  player_batting_stats AS (
    SELECT player_id,
           MAX(runs) AS highest_score,
           SUM(CASE WHEN runs >= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matches_30,
           SUM(CASE WHEN runs >= 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matches_50,
           SUM(CASE WHEN runs >= 100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matches_100
    FROM batsman_match_runs
    GROUP BY player_id
  ),
  -- Total runs scored (batsman)
  player_runs_total AS (
    SELECT b.striker AS player_id,
           SUM(bs.runs_scored) AS total_runs
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    JOIN batsman_scored bs USING(match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id)
    GROUP BY b.striker
  ),
  -- Total dismissals
  player_dismissals AS (
    SELECT player_out AS player_id,
           COUNT(*) AS dismissals
    FROM wicket_taken
    GROUP BY player_out
  ),
  -- Total balls faced
  player_balls_faced AS (
    SELECT striker AS player_id,
           COUNT(*) AS balls_faced
    FROM ball_by_ball
    GROUP BY striker
  ),
  -- Total wickets taken (bowler)
  player_wickets AS (
    SELECT b.bowler AS player_id,
           COUNT(*) AS wickets
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    JOIN wicket_taken w USING(match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id)
    GROUP BY b.bowler
  ),
  -- Total runs conceded (bowler)
  player_runs_conceded AS (
    SELECT b.bowler AS player_id,
           COALESCE(SUM(bs.runs_scored), 0) AS runs_conceded
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    LEFT JOIN batsman_scored bs USING(match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id)
    GROUP BY b.bowler
  ),
  -- Total balls bowled
  player_balls_bowled AS (
    SELECT bowler AS player_id,
           COUNT(*) AS balls_bowled
    FROM ball_by_ball
    GROUP BY bowler
  ),
  -- Total matches played
  player_matches_played AS (
    SELECT player_id,
           COUNT(DISTINCT match_id) AS matches_played
    FROM player_match
    GROUP BY player_id
  ),
  -- Per match bowling figures (wickets and runs conceded)
  bowler_match_stats AS (
    SELECT b.bowler AS player_id,
           b.match_id,
           COUNT(w.ball_id) AS wickets,
           COALESCE(SUM(bs.runs_scored), 0) AS runs_given
    FROM ball_by_ball b
    LEFT JOIN wicket_taken w USING(match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id)
    LEFT JOIN batsman_scored bs USING(match_id, innings_no, over_id, ball_id)
    GROUP BY b.bowler, b.match_id
  ),
  -- Best bowling performance per player (most wickets, fewest runs)
  best_bowling AS (
    SELECT player_id,
           wickets AS best_wickets,
           runs_given AS best_runs,
           (wickets || '-' || runs_given) AS best_bowling
    FROM (
      SELECT player_id, wickets, runs_given,
             ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY wickets DESC, runs_given ASC) AS rn
      FROM bowler_match_stats
    ) WHERE rn = 1
  )
-- Final SELECT
SELECT
  p.player_id,
  p.player_name,
  r.role AS most_frequent_role,
  p.batting_hand,
  p.bowling_skill,
  COALESCE(pr.total_runs, 0) AS total_runs,
  COALESCE(pm.matches_played, 0) AS total_matches_played,
  COALESCE(pd.dismissals, 0) AS total_dismissals,
  CASE WHEN COALESCE(pd.dismissals, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
       ELSE ROUND(pr.total_runs * 1.0 / pd.dismissals, 2) END AS batting_average,
  COALESCE(pbs.highest_score, 0) AS highest_score,
  COALESCE(pbs.matches_30, 0) AS matches_30,
  COALESCE(pbs.matches_50, 0) AS matches_50,
  COALESCE(pbs.matches_100, 0) AS matches_100,
  COALESCE(pbf.balls_faced, 0) AS total_balls_faced,
  CASE WHEN COALESCE(pbf.balls_faced, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
       ELSE ROUND(pr.total_runs * 100.0 / pbf.balls_faced, 2) END AS strike_rate,
  COALESCE(pw.wickets, 0) AS total_wickets_taken,
  CASE WHEN COALESCE(pbb.balls_bowled, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
       ELSE ROUND((COALESCE(prc.runs_conceded, 0) * 6.0) / pbb.balls_bowled, 2) END AS economy_rate,
  COALESCE(bb.best_bowling, '0-0') AS best_bowling_performance
FROM player p
LEFT JOIN ranked_roles r ON p.player_id = r.player_id AND r.rn = 1
LEFT JOIN player_runs_total pr ON p.player_id = pr.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_matches_played pm ON p.player_id = pm.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_dismissals pd ON p.player_id = pd.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_batting_stats pbs ON p.player_id = pbs.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_balls_faced pbf ON p.player_id = pbf.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_wickets pw ON p.player_id = pw.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_runs_conceded prc ON p.player_id = prc.player_id
LEFT JOIN player_balls_bowled pbb ON p.player_id = pbb.player_id
LEFT JOIN best_bowling bb ON p.player_id = bb.player_id
ORDER BY p.player_id
