WITH sol_counts AS (
    -- Count total occurrences of each problem in the solution table
    SELECT 
        name, 
        COUNT(*) AS total_sol_count
    FROM solution
    GROUP BY name
),
satisfying_pairs AS (
    -- For each problem, step, and version, identify where the condition holds:
    -- at least one 'Stack' model and at least one non-'Stack' model,
    -- and the maximum test score among non-'Stack' models is strictly less than the Stack model's test score.
    SELECT 
        ms.name,
        ms.step,
        ms.version
    FROM model_score ms
    WHERE ms.step IN (1, 2, 3)  -- only steps 1,2,3
    GROUP BY ms.name, ms.step, ms.version
    HAVING 
        -- There is at least one 'Stack' model
        SUM(CASE WHEN ms.model = 'Stack' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
        -- There is at least one non-'Stack' model
        AND SUM(CASE WHEN ms.model != 'Stack' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
        -- The highest test score among non-'Stack' models is less than the Stack model's test score
        AND MAX(CASE WHEN ms.model = 'Stack' THEN ms.test_score END) >
            MAX(CASE WHEN ms.model != 'Stack' THEN ms.test_score END)
),
satisfying_counts AS (
    -- Count how many such (step, version) pairs each problem has
    SELECT 
        name,
        COUNT(*) AS satisfy_count
    FROM satisfying_pairs
    GROUP BY name
)
-- Select problems from the problem table where the satisfy_count exceeds the total_sol_count
SELECT p.name
FROM problem p
JOIN satisfying_counts sc ON p.name = sc.name
JOIN sol_counts solc ON p.name = solc.name
WHERE sc.satisfy_count > solc.total_sol_count
pred.local263:
WITH
-- Step 1: For each (name, version, step), compute stack_test_score and max non-stack test score
step_scores AS (
    SELECT
        s.name,
        s.version,
        s.step,
        MAX(CASE WHEN ms.model = 'Stack' THEN ms.test_score END) AS stack_score,
        MAX(CASE WHEN ms.model != 'Stack' THEN ms.test_score END) AS max_non_stack
    FROM (SELECT DISTINCT name, version, step FROM model_score) AS s
    LEFT JOIN model_score ms ON ms.name = s.name AND ms.version = s.version AND ms.step = s.step
    GROUP BY s.name, s.version, s.step
    HAVING stack_score IS NOT NULL AND max_non_stack IS NOT NULL
),
-- Determine step_status
step_status AS (
    SELECT
        name,
        version,
        step,
        CASE
            WHEN max_non_stack < stack_score THEN 'strong'
            WHEN max_non_stack = stack_score THEN 'soft'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS step_status
    FROM step_scores
    WHERE step_status IS NOT NULL
),
-- Model-level status: strong if any step is strong, else soft if any step is soft
model_status AS (
    SELECT
        name,
        version,
        CASE
            WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN step_status = 'strong' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'strong'
            WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN step_status = 'soft' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'soft'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS model_status
    FROM step_status
    GROUP BY name, version
    HAVING model_status IS NOT NULL
),
-- Get the L1_model for each (name, version) (assuming constant across steps)
model_l1 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT name, version, L1_model
    FROM model
),
-- Combine status with L1_model and count occurrences
status_l1_counts AS (
    SELECT
        ms.model_status AS status,
        ml.L1_model,
        COUNT(*) AS occurrence_count
    FROM model_status ms
    JOIN model_l1 ml ON ms.name = ml.name AND ms.version = ml.version
    GROUP BY ms.model_status, ml.L1_model
),
-- Rank within each status to get the highest occurrence
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        status,
        L1_model,
        occurrence_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY status ORDER BY occurrence_count DESC) AS rn
    FROM status_l1_counts
)
SELECT
    status,
    L1_model,
    occurrence_count
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY status

FROM model
GROUP BY L1_model
ORDER BY total_count DESC

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Top-level packaging combinations (those not contained in any other packaging)
roots AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM packaging
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT contains_id FROM packaging_relations)
),
-- Leaf-level packaging items (those that do not contain any other packaging)
leaves AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM packaging
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT packaging_id FROM packaging_relations)
),
-- Recursive expansion of all root-to-leaf paths
paths AS (
    SELECT r.id AS root_id, r.id AS current_id, 1 AS qty_product
    FROM roots r
    UNION ALL
    SELECT p.root_id, pr.contains_id, p.qty_product * pr.qty
    FROM paths p
    JOIN packaging_relations pr ON p.current_id = pr.packaging_id
),
-- Total leaf quantity per root combination
leaf_totals AS (
    SELECT p.root_id, SUM(p.qty_product) AS total_leaf_qty
    FROM paths p
    WHERE p.current_id IN (SELECT id FROM leaves)
    GROUP BY p.root_id
)
-- Average total leaf quantity across all final combinations
SELECT AVG(total_leaf_qty) AS avg_total_leaf_qty
FROM leaf_totals

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Start with top-level packaging (not contained in any other)
    SELECT 
        p.id AS top_level_id,
        p.name AS top_level_name,
        p.id AS item_id,
        p.name AS item_name,
        1.0 AS qty
    FROM packaging p
    WHERE p.id NOT IN (SELECT contains_id FROM packaging_relations)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recurse down: multiply quantities along the containment chain
    SELECT 
        h.top_level_id,
        h.top_level_name,
        pr.contains_id AS item_id,
        p2.name AS item_name,
        h.qty * pr.qty AS qty
    FROM hierarchy h
    JOIN packaging_relations pr ON h.item_id = pr.packaging_id
    JOIN packaging p2 ON pr.contains_id = p2.id
),
aggregated AS (
    -- Sum quantities for each (top-level container, item) pair across all paths
    SELECT 
        top_level_id,
        top_level_name,
        item_id,
        item_name,
        SUM(qty) AS total_qty
    FROM hierarchy
    WHERE item_id != top_level_id  -- exclude the container itself
    GROUP BY top_level_id, top_level_name, item_id, item_name
    HAVING SUM(qty) > 500
)
SELECT 
    top_level_name AS container_name,
    item_name
FROM aggregated
ORDER BY container_name, item_name
pred.local272:
-- This query computes the picking instructions for order 423.
-- It uses a range-based allocation method to assign inventory to order lines,
-- respecting the sequence of order lines (by rowid) and FIFO + smallest-quantity-first prioritization.
WITH
-- Step 1: Extract order lines for order 423, assign a sequence using rowid
order_lines AS (
    SELECT
        ol.product_id,
        ol.qty,
        ol.rowid AS seq
    FROM orderlines ol
    WHERE ol.order_id = 423
),

-- Step 2: For each product, compute cumulative required quantities over order lines
ordered_lines AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        qty,
        seq,
        COALESCE(
            SUM(qty) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY seq ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING),
            0
        ) AS cum_req_start,
        SUM(qty) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY seq) AS cum_req_end
    FROM order_lines
),

-- Step 3: Get inventory for the products in order 423, located in warehouse 1
-- Join with purchases for date, and locations for aisle/position.
-- Compute cumulative inventory amounts, ordering by purchased (ASC) then quantity (ASC) then inventory.id (stable tiebreak).
inventory_data AS (
    SELECT
        i.product_id,
        i.qty,
        i.id AS inv_id,
        l.aisle,
        l.position,
        p.purchased,
        COALESCE(
            SUM(i.qty) OVER (PARTITION BY i.product_id ORDER BY p.purchased, i.qty, i.id ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING),
            0
        ) AS cum_inv_start,
        SUM(i.qty) OVER (PARTITION BY i.product_id ORDER BY p.purchased, i.qty, i.id) AS cum_inv_end
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN purchases p ON i.purchase_id = p.id
    JOIN locations l ON i.location_id = l.id
    WHERE l.warehouse = 1
      AND i.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM order_lines)
),

-- Step 4: Identify overlapping intervals between order lines and inventory
-- Compute the quantity to pick from each inventory record for each order line.
allocations AS (
    SELECT
        ol.product_id,
        ol.cum_req_start,
        ol.cum_req_end,
        inv.inv_id,
        inv.aisle,
        inv.position,
        inv.cum_inv_start,
        inv.cum_inv_end,
        -- Overlap length
        CASE
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end < inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start > inv.cum_inv_start THEN ol.cum_req_end - ol.cum_req_start
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end >= inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start <= inv.cum_inv_start THEN inv.cum_inv_end - inv.cum_inv_start
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end < inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start <= inv.cum_inv_start THEN ol.cum_req_end - inv.cum_inv_start
            WHEN ol.cum_req_end >= inv.cum_inv_end AND ol.cum_req_start > inv.cum_inv_start THEN inv.cum_inv_end - ol.cum_req_start
            ELSE 0
        END AS pick_qty
    FROM ordered_lines ol
    JOIN inventory_data inv ON ol.product_id = inv.product_id
    WHERE ol.cum_req_start < inv.cum_inv_end
      AND ol.cum_req_end > inv.cum_inv_start
),

-- Step 5: Aggregate picks per product and per location (sum over order lines)
picking_instructions AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        aisle,
        position,
        SUM(pick_qty) AS total_pick_qty
    FROM allocations
    WHERE pick_qty > 0
    GROUP BY product_id, aisle, position
)

-- Final output: product IDs, aisles, and positions (and optionally the quantity)
-- According to the question, only the three columns are requested.
-- Uncomment the total_pick_qty if quantities are also desired.
SELECT
    product_id,
    aisle,
    position
    -- , total_pick_qty   -- uncomment to include the quantity to pick from each location
FROM picking_instructions
ORDER BY product_id, aisle, position
pred.local273:
WITH
-- Combine inventory and order events into a single timeline per product
events AS (
    SELECT
        i.product_id,
        p.purchased AS event_date,
        -- Sorting: 0 for inventory (so it comes before orders on the same date)
        0 AS event_type_sort,
        i.qty AS qty,
        i.id AS source_id,
        1 AS is_inventory,
        i.qty AS inv_qty,
        NULL AS order_qty
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN purchases p ON i.purchase_id = p.id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        ol.product_id,
        o.ordered AS event_date,
        -- Sorting: 1 for orders (after inventory on same date)
        1 AS event_type_sort,
        ol.qty AS qty,
        o.id AS source_id,
        0 AS is_inventory,
        NULL AS inv_qty,
        ol.qty AS order_qty
    FROM orderlines ol
    JOIN orders o ON ol.order_id = o.id
),
-- Add a global event order per product respecting FIFO rules
events_with_order AS (
    SELECT *,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY product_id
            ORDER BY event_date,
                     event_type_sort,
                     CASE WHEN is_inventory = 1 THEN qty ELSE NULL END,  -- smallest qty first
                     source_id
        ) AS event_rank
    FROM events
),
-- Compute cumulative inventory and cumulative demand before each event
cumulative AS (
    SELECT *,
        COALESCE(SUM(inv_qty) OVER (
            PARTITION BY product_id
            ORDER BY event_rank
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
        ), 0) AS cum_inv_before,
        COALESCE(SUM(order_qty) OVER (
            PARTITION BY product_id
            ORDER BY event_rank
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
        ), 0) AS cum_demand_before
    FROM events_with_order
),
-- For each order event, compute the picked quantity using FIFO
order_alloc AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        source_id AS order_id,
        order_qty,
        event_date,
        cum_inv_before,
        cum_demand_before,
        CASE
            WHEN cum_inv_before - cum_demand_before <= 0 THEN 0
            ELSE MIN(order_qty, cum_inv_before - cum_demand_before)
        END AS picked_qty
    FROM cumulative
    WHERE is_inventory = 0  -- only order rows
),
-- Compute pick percentage per order line
order_pct AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        order_id,
        1.0 * picked_qty / order_qty AS pick_pct
    FROM order_alloc
    WHERE order_qty > 0
)
-- Final aggregation by product name
SELECT
    pg.name AS product_name,
    AVG(op.pick_pct) AS avg_pick_percentage
FROM order_pct op
JOIN products p ON op.product_id = p.id
JOIN product_groups pg ON p.group_id = pg.id
GROUP BY pg.name
ORDER BY pg.name

WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT mth, product_id, qty
  FROM monthly_sales
  WHERE mth >= '2016-01-01'
),
ma12 AS (
  SELECT mth, product_id, qty,
         AVG(qty) OVER (
           PARTITION BY product_id 
           ORDER BY mth 
           ROWS BETWEEN 5 PRECEDING AND 6 FOLLOWING
         ) AS ma12
  FROM monthly
),
cma AS (
  SELECT mth, product_id, qty, ma12,
         (ma12 + LAG(ma12) OVER (
           PARTITION BY product_id 
           ORDER BY mth
         )) / 2.0 AS cma
  FROM ma12
),
ratios AS (
  SELECT mth, product_id, qty / cma AS ratio
  FROM cma
  WHERE cma IS NOT NULL
),
year2017 AS (
  SELECT product_id, mth, ratio
  FROM ratios
  WHERE strftime('%Y', mth) = '2017'
)
SELECT product_id
FROM year2017
GROUP BY product_id
HAVING COUNT(*) = 12 
   AND MIN(ratio) > 2
pred.local277:
WITH RECURSIVE months(mth) AS (
    SELECT '2016-01-01' AS mth
    UNION ALL
    SELECT date(mth, '+1 month') 
    FROM months 
    WHERE mth < '2019-01-01'
),
target_products AS (
    SELECT 4160 AS product_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 7790
),
base AS (
    SELECT 
        p.product_id,
        m.mth,
        COALESCE(s.qty, 0.0) AS qty
    FROM months m
    CROSS JOIN target_products p
    LEFT JOIN monthly_sales s 
        ON s.product_id = p.product_id 
        AND s.mth = m.mth
),
with_time AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        mth,
        qty,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY mth) AS time_step
    FROM base
),
seasonal_data AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        CAST(strftime('%m', mth) AS INTEGER) AS month_num,
        AVG(qty) AS avg_month,
        (SELECT AVG(qty) FROM with_time WHERE product_id = t.product_id AND time_step BETWEEN 7 AND 30) AS grand_avg
    FROM with_time t
    WHERE time_step BETWEEN 7 AND 30
    GROUP BY product_id, month_num
),
seasonal_factors AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        month_num,
        CASE WHEN grand_avg = 0 THEN 1 ELSE avg_month / grand_avg END AS season_factor
    FROM seasonal_data
),
deseq AS (
    SELECT 
        t.product_id,
        t.time_step,
        t.qty,
        COALESCE(sf.season_factor, 1) AS season_factor,
        CASE 
            WHEN COALESCE(sf.season_factor, 1) <> 0 
            THEN t.qty / COALESCE(sf.season_factor, 1) 
            ELSE t.qty 
        END AS deseasonalized
    FROM with_time t
    JOIN seasonal_factors sf 
        ON sf.product_id = t.product_id 
        AND sf.month_num = CAST(strftime('%m', t.mth) AS INTEGER)
),
regression AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        SUM(time_step) AS sum_w,
        SUM(time_step * time_step) AS sum_wx,
        SUM(time_step * deseasonalized) AS sum_wy,
        SUM(time_step * time_step * time_step) AS sum_wx2,
        SUM(time_step * time_step * deseasonalized) AS sum_wxy
    FROM deseq
    GROUP BY product_id
),
coeffs AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        (sum_w * sum_wxy - sum_wx * sum_wy) * 1.0 / 
            NULLIF((sum_w * sum_wx2 - sum_wx * sum_wx), 0) AS slope,
        (sum_wy - ((sum_w * sum_wxy - sum_wx * sum_wy) * 1.0 / 
            NULLIF((sum_w * sum_wx2 - sum_wx * sum_wx), 0)) * sum_wx) / 
            NULLIF(sum_w, 0) AS intercept
    FROM regression
),
forecast_months AS (
    SELECT product_id, time_step
    FROM with_time
    WHERE time_step BETWEEN 25 AND 36
    GROUP BY product_id, time_step
),
forecast AS (
    SELECT 
        f.product_id,
        f.time_step,
        c.intercept + c.slope * f.time_step AS pred_deseasonalized,
        sf.season_factor
    FROM forecast_months f
    JOIN coeffs c ON c.product_id = f.product_id
    LEFT JOIN seasonal_factors sf 
        ON sf.product_id = f.product_id
        AND sf.month_num = ((f.time_step - 1) % 12) + 1
),
annual_forecast AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        SUM(pred_deseasonalized * season_factor) AS annual_sales
    FROM forecast
    GROUP BY product_id
)
SELECT AVG(annual_sales) AS avg_forecasted_annual_sales_2018
FROM annual_forecast
pred.local279:
WITH RECURSIVE months AS (
  SELECT '2019-01-01' AS mth
  UNION ALL
  SELECT date(mth, '+1 month')
  FROM months
  WHERE mth < '2019-12-01'
),
-- All product-month combinations with sales (0 if none)
sales_data AS (
  SELECT p.product_id, m.mth, COALESCE(s.qty, 0) AS sales_qty
  FROM (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM inventory) p
  CROSS JOIN months m
  LEFT JOIN monthly_sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id AND s.mth = m.mth
),
starting_inv AS (
  SELECT product_id, qty AS start_qty FROM inventory
),
minima AS (
  SELECT product_id, qty_minimum FROM product_minimums
),
-- Recursive simulation month by month
inventory_sim AS (
  -- January 2019: starting inventory = December 2018 level
  SELECT 
    s.product_id,
    s.mth,
    i.start_qty AS starting_inv,
    s.sales_qty,
    i.start_qty - s.sales_qty AS before_restock,
    m.qty_minimum,
    CASE WHEN i.start_qty - s.sales_qty < m.qty_minimum THEN m.qty_minimum ELSE i.start_qty - s.sales_qty END AS ending_inv,
    ABS(CASE WHEN i.start_qty - s.sales_qty < m.qty_minimum THEN m.qty_minimum ELSE i.start_qty - s.sales_qty END - m.qty_minimum) AS diff
  FROM sales_data s
  JOIN starting_inv i ON s.product_id = i.product_id
  JOIN minima m ON s.product_id = m.product_id
  WHERE s.mth = '2019-01-01'

  UNION ALL

  -- Subsequent months: start with previous ending inventory
  SELECT
    s.product_id,
    s.mth,
    sim.ending_inv,
    s.sales_qty,
    sim.ending_inv - s.sales_qty AS before_restock,
    m.qty_minimum,
    CASE WHEN sim.ending_inv - s.sales_qty < m.qty_minimum THEN m.qty_minimum ELSE sim.ending_inv - s.sales_qty END AS ending_inv,
    ABS(CASE WHEN sim.ending_inv - s.sales_qty < m.qty_minimum THEN m.qty_minimum ELSE sim.ending_inv - s.sales_qty END - m.qty_minimum) AS diff
  FROM sales_data s
  JOIN inventory_sim sim ON s.product_id = sim.product_id AND s.mth = date(sim.mth, '+1 month')
  JOIN minima m ON s.product_id = m.product_id
)
-- For each product, pick the month with the smallest absolute difference (ties broken by earliest month)
SELECT product_id, mth, diff
FROM (
  SELECT product_id, mth, diff,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY diff ASC, mth ASC) AS rn
  FROM inventory_sim
)
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY product_id

WITH match_points AS (
    SELECT 
        season,
        league_id,
        home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN home_team_goal > away_team_goal THEN 3
            WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS points
    FROM Match
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        season,
        league_id,
        away_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN away_team_goal > home_team_goal THEN 3
            WHEN away_team_goal = home_team_goal THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS points
    FROM Match
),
team_season_points AS (
    SELECT 
        season,
        league_id,
        team_api_id,
        SUM(points) AS total_points
    FROM match_points
    GROUP BY season, league_id, team_api_id
),
ranked_teams AS (
    SELECT 
        season,
        league_id,
        team_api_id,
        total_points,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY season, league_id 
            ORDER BY total_points DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM team_season_points
)
SELECT 
    r.season,
    t.team_long_name AS champion_team,
    l.name AS league,
    c.name AS country,
    r.total_points
FROM ranked_teams r
JOIN Team t ON r.team_api_id = t.team_api_id
JOIN League l ON r.league_id = l.id
JOIN Country c ON l.country_id = c.id
WHERE r.rn = 1
ORDER BY r.season, c.name

WITH 
mean AS (
    SELECT AVG("loss_rate_%") AS avg_loss
    FROM veg_loss_rate_df
),
std AS (
    SELECT SQRT(AVG(("loss_rate_%" - m.avg_loss) * ("loss_rate_%" - m.avg_loss))) AS std_loss
    FROM veg_loss_rate_df, mean m
),
categorized AS (
    SELECT 
        v."loss_rate_%",
        CASE 
            WHEN v."loss_rate_%" < m.avg_loss - s.std_loss THEN 'Below'
            WHEN v."loss_rate_%" > m.avg_loss + s.std_loss THEN 'Above'
            ELSE 'Within'
        END AS category
    FROM veg_loss_rate_df v
    CROSS JOIN mean m
    CROSS JOIN std s
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT avg_loss FROM mean) AS average_loss_rate,
    SUM(CASE WHEN category = 'Below' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS below_one_std,
    SUM(CASE WHEN category = 'Within' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS within_one_std,
    SUM(CASE WHEN category = 'Above' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS above_one_std
FROM categorized
pred.local285:
-- Step 1: Wholesale price statistics per category per year
WITH wholesale_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        c.category_name,
        strftime('%Y', w.whsle_date) AS year,
        ROUND(AVG(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg), 2) AS avg_wholesale_price,
        ROUND(MAX(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg), 2) AS max_wholesale_price,
        ROUND(MIN(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg), 2) AS min_wholesale_price,
        ROUND(MAX(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg) - MIN(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg), 2) AS wholesale_price_difference,
        ROUND(SUM(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg), 2) AS total_wholesale_price
    FROM veg_whsle_df w
    JOIN veg_cat c ON w.item_code = c.item_code
    WHERE strftime('%Y', w.whsle_date) BETWEEN '2020' AND '2023'
    GROUP BY c.category_name, year
),

-- Step 2: Transaction-based aggregated metrics (only sales)
txn_agg AS (
    SELECT 
        c.category_name,
        strftime('%Y', t.txn_date) AS year,
        ROUND(SUM(t.unit_selling_px_rmb_kg * t.qty_sold_kg), 2) AS total_selling_price,
        ROUND(SUM(w.whsle_px_rmb_kg * t.qty_sold_kg), 2) AS total_wholesale_cost,
        ROUND(AVG(l.loss_rate_), 2) AS avg_loss_rate,
        ROUND(SUM((l.loss_rate_ / 100.0) * (w.whsle_px_rmb_kg * t.qty_sold_kg)), 2) AS total_loss
    FROM veg_txn_df t
    JOIN veg_whsle_df w ON t.item_code = w.item_code AND t.txn_date = w.whsle_date
    JOIN veg_cat c ON t.item_code = c.item_code
    JOIN veg_loss_rate_df l ON t.item_code = l.item_code
    WHERE t.sale_return = 'sale'
      AND strftime('%Y', t.txn_date) BETWEEN '2020' AND '2023'
    GROUP BY c.category_name, year
)

-- Final selection: combine wholesale stats with transaction metrics
SELECT 
    ws.category_name,
    ws.year,
    ws.avg_wholesale_price,
    ws.max_wholesale_price,
    ws.min_wholesale_price,
    ws.wholesale_price_difference,
    ws.total_wholesale_price,
    ta.total_selling_price,
    ta.avg_loss_rate,
    ta.total_loss,
    ROUND(ta.total_selling_price - ta.total_wholesale_cost - ta.total_loss, 2) AS profit
FROM wholesale_stats ws
LEFT JOIN txn_agg ta ON ws.category_name = ta.category_name AND ws.year = ta.year
ORDER BY ws.category_name, ws.year

WITH seller_sales AS (
    SELECT
        oi.seller_id,
        SUM(oi.price) AS total_sales,
        AVG(oi.price) AS avg_item_price,
        COUNT(*) AS total_quantity,
        AVG(julianday(oi.shipping_limit_date) - julianday(o.order_purchase_timestamp)) AS avg_packing_days
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
    GROUP BY oi.seller_id
),
seller_reviews AS (
    SELECT
        oi.seller_id,
        AVG(CAST(r.review_score AS REAL)) AS avg_review_score
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
    JOIN order_reviews r ON o.order_id = r.order_id
    GROUP BY oi.seller_id
),
seller_category_volume AS (
    SELECT
        oi.seller_id,
        p.product_category_name AS cat_portuguese,
        COUNT(*) AS cat_quantity
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY oi.seller_id, p.product_category_name
),
ranked_categories AS (
    SELECT
        scv.seller_id,
        scv.cat_portuguese,
        scv.cat_quantity,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY scv.seller_id ORDER BY scv.cat_quantity DESC) AS rn
    FROM seller_category_volume scv
),
top_category AS (
    SELECT
        rc.seller_id,
        t.product_category_name_english AS top_sales_volume_category
    FROM ranked_categories rc
    JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON rc.cat_portuguese = t.product_category_name
    WHERE rc.rn = 1
)
SELECT
    ss.seller_id,
    ss.total_sales,
    ss.avg_item_price,
    sr.avg_review_score,
    ss.avg_packing_days AS avg_packing_time,
    tc.top_sales_volume_category
FROM seller_sales ss
LEFT JOIN seller_reviews sr ON ss.seller_id = sr.seller_id
LEFT JOIN top_category tc ON ss.seller_id = tc.seller_id
WHERE ss.total_quantity > 100
ORDER BY ss.total_sales DESC

WITH years(y) AS (
  VALUES (2018), (2019), (2020)
),
-- Compute the Monday of the week containing June 15 for each year
monday_dates AS (
  SELECT 
    y,
    date(y || '-06-15') AS june15,
    date(date(y || '-06-15'), 
         '-' || ((strftime('%w', date(y || '-06-15')) + 6) % 7) || ' days') AS monday
  FROM years
),
-- Classify each week as "before" (4 weeks prior) or "after" (4 weeks after)
period_sales AS (
  SELECT 
    md.y,
    CASE 
      WHEN cws.week_date >= date(md.monday, '-28 days') 
       AND cws.week_date < md.monday THEN 'before'
      WHEN cws.week_date > md.monday 
       AND cws.week_date <= date(md.monday, '+28 days') THEN 'after'
    END AS period,
    cws.sales
  FROM cleaned_weekly_sales cws
  JOIN monday_dates md ON cws.calendar_year = md.y
  WHERE (cws.week_date >= date(md.monday, '-28 days') AND cws.week_date < md.monday)
     OR (cws.week_date > md.monday AND cws.week_date <= date(md.monday, '+28 days'))
),
-- Sum sales per year and period
aggregated AS (
  SELECT y, period, SUM(sales) AS total_sales
  FROM period_sales
  WHERE period IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY y, period
)
-- Compute percentage change
SELECT 
  a.y AS year,
  SUM(CASE WHEN a.period = 'before' THEN a.total_sales END) AS sales_before,
  SUM(CASE WHEN a.period = 'after' THEN a.total_sales END) AS sales_after,
  ROUND(
    (SUM(CASE WHEN a.period = 'after' THEN a.total_sales END) - 
     SUM(CASE WHEN a.period = 'before' THEN a.total_sales END)) * 100.0 / 
    SUM(CASE WHEN a.period = 'before' THEN a.total_sales END), 2
  ) AS pct_change
FROM aggregated a
GROUP BY a.y
ORDER BY a.y
pred.local302:
WITH 
-- Before sales by attribute values
before_region AS (
    SELECT region AS value, SUM(sales) AS before_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date >= '2020-03-23' AND week_date < '2020-06-15'
    GROUP BY region
),
before_platform AS (
    SELECT platform AS value, SUM(sales) AS before_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date >= '2020-03-23' AND week_date < '2020-06-15'
    GROUP BY platform
),
before_age_band AS (
    SELECT age_band AS value, SUM(sales) AS before_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date >= '2020-03-23' AND week_date < '2020-06-15'
    GROUP BY age_band
),
before_demographic AS (
    SELECT demographic AS value, SUM(sales) AS before_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date >= '2020-03-23' AND week_date < '2020-06-15'
    GROUP BY demographic
),
before_customer_type AS (
    SELECT customer_type AS value, SUM(sales) AS before_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date >= '2020-03-23' AND week_date < '2020-06-15'
    GROUP BY customer_type
),
-- After sales by attribute values
after_region AS (
    SELECT region AS value, SUM(sales) AS after_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date > '2020-06-15' AND week_date <= '2020-09-07'
    GROUP BY region
),
after_platform AS (
    SELECT platform AS value, SUM(sales) AS after_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date > '2020-06-15' AND week_date <= '2020-09-07'
    GROUP BY platform
),
after_age_band AS (
    SELECT age_band AS value, SUM(sales) AS after_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date > '2020-06-15' AND week_date <= '2020-09-07'
    GROUP BY age_band
),
after_demographic AS (
    SELECT demographic AS value, SUM(sales) AS after_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date > '2020-06-15' AND week_date <= '2020-09-07'
    GROUP BY demographic
),
after_customer_type AS (
    SELECT customer_type AS value, SUM(sales) AS after_sales
    FROM cleaned_weekly_sales
    WHERE week_date > '2020-06-15' AND week_date <= '2020-09-07'
    GROUP BY customer_type
),
-- Percentage change per attribute value for each type
region_pct AS (
    SELECT b.value, 
           (a.after_sales - b.before_sales) * 100.0 / b.before_sales AS pct_change
    FROM before_region b
    JOIN after_region a ON b.value = a.value
    WHERE b.before_sales <> 0
),
platform_pct AS (
    SELECT b.value,
           (a.after_sales - b.before_sales) * 100.0 / b.before_sales AS pct_change
    FROM before_platform b
    JOIN after_platform a ON b.value = a.value
    WHERE b.before_sales <> 0
),
age_band_pct AS (
    SELECT b.value,
           (a.after_sales - b.before_sales) * 100.0 / b.before_sales AS pct_change
    FROM before_age_band b
    JOIN after_age_band a ON b.value = a.value
    WHERE b.before_sales <> 0
),
demographic_pct AS (
    SELECT b.value,
           (a.after_sales - b.before_sales) * 100.0 / b.before_sales AS pct_change
    FROM before_demographic b
    JOIN after_demographic a ON b.value = a.value
    WHERE b.before_sales <> 0
),
customer_type_pct AS (
    SELECT b.value,
           (a.after_sales - b.before_sales) * 100.0 / b.before_sales AS pct_change
    FROM before_customer_type b
    JOIN after_customer_type a ON b.value = a.value
    WHERE b.before_sales <> 0
),
-- Average percentage change per attribute type
region_avg AS (
    SELECT 'region' AS attribute_type, AVG(pct_change) AS avg_pct_change
    FROM region_pct
),
platform_avg AS (
    SELECT 'platform' AS attribute_type, AVG(pct_change) AS avg_pct_change
    FROM platform_pct
),
age_band_avg AS (
    SELECT 'age_band' AS attribute_type, AVG(pct_change) AS avg_pct_change
    FROM age_band_pct
),
demographic_avg AS (
    SELECT 'demographic' AS attribute_type, AVG(pct_change) AS avg_pct_change
    FROM demographic_pct
),
customer_type_avg AS (
    SELECT 'customer_type' AS attribute_type, AVG(pct_change) AS avg_pct_change
    FROM customer_type_pct
),
all_avg AS (
    SELECT * FROM region_avg
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM platform_avg
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM age_band_avg
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM demographic_avg
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM customer_type_avg
)
SELECT attribute_type, avg_pct_change
FROM all_avg
ORDER BY avg_pct_change ASC
LIMIT 1

WITH input_visits AS (
    SELECT session, stamp
    FROM form_log
    WHERE path = '/regist/input'
),
confirm_visits AS (
    SELECT session, stamp
    FROM form_log
    WHERE path = '/regist/confirm'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT i.session) AS unique_sessions
FROM input_visits i
JOIN confirm_visits c ON i.session = c.session
WHERE i.stamp < c.stamp
pred.local330:
WITH 
-- Step 1: For each session, identify the first page (landing) based on earliest stamp
first_page AS (
    SELECT session, path AS landing_page
    FROM (
        SELECT session, path, stamp,
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp ASC) AS rn
        FROM activity_log
    ) sub
    WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Step 2: For each session, identify the last page (exit) based on latest stamp
last_page AS (
    SELECT session, path AS exit_page
    FROM (
        SELECT session, path, stamp,
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp DESC) AS rn
        FROM activity_log
    ) sub
    WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Step 3: Combine unique (session, page) pairs where the page is either landing or exit
combined AS (
    SELECT session, landing_page AS page FROM first_page
    UNION
    SELECT session, exit_page AS page FROM last_page
)
-- Step 4: Count distinct sessions per web page
SELECT page AS web_page, COUNT(DISTINCT session) AS unique_sessions
FROM combined
GROUP BY page
ORDER BY page
pred.local331:
WITH ordered_visits AS (
    SELECT
        session,
        stamp,
        path,
        LAG(path, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp) AS prev1,
        LAG(path, 2) OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp) AS prev2
    FROM activity_log
    WHERE action = 'view'
),
patterns AS (
    SELECT
        session,
        stamp,
        path AS third_page
    FROM ordered_visits
    WHERE prev1 = '/detail/'
      AND prev2 = '/detail/'
)
SELECT
    third_page,
    COUNT(*) AS frequency
FROM patterns
GROUP BY third_page
ORDER BY frequency DESC
LIMIT 3

WITH user_ages AS (
  SELECT user_id,
         CAST((strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', birth_date)) - 
              (strftime('%m-%d', 'now') < strftime('%m-%d', birth_date)) AS INTEGER) AS age
  FROM mst_users
)
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN age BETWEEN 20 AND 29 THEN '20s'
    WHEN age BETWEEN 30 AND 39 THEN '30s'
    WHEN age BETWEEN 40 AND 49 THEN '40s'
    WHEN age BETWEEN 50 AND 59 THEN '50s'
    ELSE 'others'
  END AS age_category,
  COUNT(*) AS user_count
FROM user_ages
GROUP BY age_category
ORDER BY age_category
pred.local360:
WITH first_event AS (
    -- For each session, get the first occurrence of '/detail' or '/complete'
    SELECT 
        session,
        stamp AS first_stamp,
        path AS first_path,
        search_type AS first_search_type
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            session,
            stamp,
            path,
            search_type,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY session ORDER BY stamp, path) AS rn
        FROM activity_log
        WHERE path IN ('/detail', '/complete')
    ) t
    WHERE rn = 1
),
pre_events_count AS (
    -- Count events with non‑empty search_type before the first '/detail' or '/complete'
    SELECT 
        fe.session,
        fe.first_path,
        fe.first_search_type,
        COUNT(al.stamp) AS cnt
    FROM first_event fe
    LEFT JOIN activity_log al 
        ON al.session = fe.session 
        AND al.search_type IS NOT NULL 
        AND al.search_type != ''
        AND al.stamp < fe.first_stamp
    GROUP BY fe.session, fe.first_path, fe.first_search_type
),
min_count AS (
    -- Find the minimum count among all sessions
    SELECT MIN(cnt) AS min_cnt
    FROM pre_events_count
)
-- Return sessions with the minimum count along with the path and search_type of the first event
SELECT 
    pec.session,
    pec.first_path AS path,
    pec.first_search_type AS search_type
FROM pre_events_count pec
JOIN min_count mc ON pec.cnt = mc.min_cnt
ORDER BY pec.session
pred.local344:
WITH
    -- Step 1: Select races that have pit stop data available
    races_with_pit AS (
        SELECT race_id
        FROM races_ext
        WHERE is_pit_data_available = '1'
    ),

    -- Step 2: Enrich lap positions with driver details, pit-stop flags and retirement flags
    lap_enriched AS (
        SELECT
            l.race_id,
            l.driver_id,
            d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS driver_name,
            l.lap,
            l.position,
            l.lap_type,
            CASE WHEN p.driver_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_pit_stop,
            CASE WHEN r.driver_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_retirement
        FROM lap_positions l
        JOIN drivers d ON l.driver_id = d.driver_id
        JOIN races_with_pit rp ON l.race_id = rp.race_id
        LEFT JOIN pit_stops p
            ON p.race_id = l.race_id AND p.driver_id = l.driver_id AND p.lap = l.lap
        LEFT JOIN retirements r
            ON r.race_id = l.race_id AND r.driver_id = l.driver_id AND CAST(r.lap AS INTEGER) = l.lap
    ),

    -- Step 3: For each driver and race, bring the previous lap's position and flags
    laps_with_prev AS (
        SELECT
            race_id,
            driver_id,
            driver_name,
            lap,
            position,
            lap_type,
            has_pit_stop,
            has_retirement,
            LAG(lap) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_lap,
            LAG(position) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_position,
            LAG(has_pit_stop) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_has_pit_stop,
            LAG(has_retirement) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap) AS prev_has_retirement
        FROM lap_enriched
    ),

    -- Step 4: Keep only consecutive laps (previous lap exists and exactly one less)
    consecutive_laps AS (
        SELECT
            race_id,
            driver_id,
            driver_name,
            lap AS curr_lap,
            position AS curr_pos,
            prev_position AS prev_pos,
            has_pit_stop AS curr_pit,
            has_retirement AS curr_ret,
            prev_has_pit_stop AS prev_pit,
            prev_has_retirement AS prev_ret,
            lap_type
        FROM laps_with_prev
        WHERE prev_lap IS NOT NULL AND prev_lap = lap - 1
    ),

    -- Step 5: Detect overtakes between pairs of drivers on the same lap
    overtake_events AS (
        SELECT
            a.race_id,
            a.curr_lap,
            a.driver_name AS driver_a,
            b.driver_name AS driver_b,
            a.curr_pos AS pos_a_curr,
            b.curr_pos AS pos_b_curr,
            a.prev_pos AS pos_a_prev,
            b.prev_pos AS pos_b_prev,
            a.curr_pit AS a_pit,
            b.curr_pit AS b_pit,
            a.curr_ret AS a_ret,
            b.curr_ret AS b_ret,
            a.prev_pit AS a_prev_pit,
            b.prev_pit AS b_prev_pit,
            a.prev_ret AS a_prev_ret,
            b.prev_ret AS b_prev_ret,
            a.lap_type
        FROM consecutive_laps a
        JOIN consecutive_laps b
            ON a.race_id = b.race_id
            AND a.curr_lap = b.curr_lap
            AND a.driver_id < b.driver_id
        WHERE 
            -- driver a was ahead on previous lap and behind on current lap => overtaken by b
            (a.prev_pos < b.prev_pos AND a.curr_pos > b.curr_pos)
            OR
            -- driver b was ahead on previous lap and behind on current lap => overtaken by a
            (b.prev_pos < a.prev_pos AND b.curr_pos > a.curr_pos)
    ),

    -- Step 6: Classify each overtake and count
    classified AS (
        SELECT
            CASE
                WHEN curr_lap = 1 THEN 'Start Overtake'
                WHEN a_pit = 1 OR b_pit = 1 OR a_prev_pit = 1 OR b_prev_pit = 1 THEN 'Pit Stop Overtake'
                WHEN a_ret = 1 OR b_ret = 1 OR a_prev_ret = 1 OR b_prev_ret = 1 THEN 'Retirement Overtake'
                ELSE 'On-Track Overtake'
            END AS overtake_type,
            1 AS cnt
        FROM overtake_events
        -- Ensure no retirement or pit stop on the current lap for the overtaking driver? Already filtered by CASE.
    )

-- Final result
SELECT
    overtake_type,
    COUNT(*) AS number_of_overtakes
FROM classified
GROUP BY overtake_type
ORDER BY overtake_type
pred.local336:
WITH pos AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, 
         MAX(CASE WHEN lap_type LIKE 'Starting%' THEN 0 ELSE lap END) AS lap_num,
         MAX(position) AS position
  FROM lap_positions
  WHERE (lap_type = 'Race' AND lap BETWEEN 1 AND 5)
     OR (lap_type LIKE 'Starting%' AND lap = 0) -- assume lap=0 for grid
  GROUP BY race_id, driver_id, 
           CASE WHEN lap_type LIKE 'Starting%' THEN 0 ELSE lap END
),
pos_with_prev AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap_num, position,
         LAG(position) OVER (PARTITION BY race_id, driver_id ORDER BY lap_num) AS prev_pos
  FROM pos
),
gains AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap_num, position, prev_pos,
         prev_pos - position AS total_gain
  FROM pos_with_prev
  WHERE prev_pos IS NOT NULL AND prev_pos > position
),
retirements_per_lap AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, CAST(lap AS INT) AS lap_num
  FROM retirements
  WHERE CAST(lap AS INT) BETWEEN 1 AND 5
),
retirement_prev_pos AS (
  SELECT r.race_id, r.driver_id AS retired_driver, r.lap_num,
         p.position AS retired_prev_pos
  FROM retirements_per_lap r
  JOIN pos p ON r.race_id = p.race_id AND r.driver_id = p.driver_id
            AND p.lap_num = r.lap_num - 1
),
retirement_caused AS (
  SELECT g.race_id, g.driver_id, g.lap_num, g.total_gain, g.prev_pos,
         COUNT(DISTINCT r.retired_driver) AS retirement_count
  FROM gains g
  LEFT JOIN retirement_prev_pos r 
         ON g.race_id = r.race_id AND g.lap_num = r.lap_num
        AND r.retired_prev_pos < g.prev_pos
  GROUP BY g.race_id, g.driver_id, g.lap_num, g.total_gain, g.prev_pos
),
pit_stops_per_lap AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap
  FROM pit_stops
  WHERE lap BETWEEN 1 AND 5
),
pit_stop_prev_pos AS (
  SELECT p.race_id, p.driver_id AS pitting_driver, p.lap AS lap_num,
         pos.position AS pitting_prev_pos
  FROM pit_stops_per_lap p
  JOIN pos ON p.race_id = pos.race_id AND p.driver_id = pos.driver_id
           AND pos.lap_num = p.lap - 1
),
pit_stop_caused AS (
  SELECT g.race_id, g.driver_id, g.lap_num, g.total_gain, g.prev_pos,
         g.retirement_count,
         COUNT(DISTINCT p.pitting_driver) AS pit_stop_count
  FROM retirement_caused g
  LEFT JOIN pit_stop_prev_pos p
         ON g.race_id = p.race_id AND g.lap_num = p.lap_num
        AND p.pitting_prev_pos < g.prev_pos
  GROUP BY g.race_id, g.driver_id, g.lap_num, g.total_gain, g.prev_pos, g.retirement_count
),
categorized AS (
  SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap_num, total_gain,
         retirement_count, pit_stop_count,
         CASE 
            WHEN lap_num = 1 THEN 
                total_gain - retirement_count - pit_stop_count
            ELSE 0 
         END AS start_gain,
         retirement_count AS retirement_gain,
         pit_stop_count AS pit_stop_gain,
         total_gain - retirement_count - pit_stop_count -
            CASE WHEN lap_num = 1 THEN total_gain - retirement_count - pit_stop_count ELSE 0 END
            AS standard_gain
  FROM pit_stop_caused
)
SELECT 'start' AS category, SUM(start_gain) AS overtake_count
FROM categorized
UNION ALL
SELECT 'retirement', SUM(retirement_gain)
FROM categorized
UNION ALL
SELECT 'pit stop', SUM(pit_stop_gain)
FROM categorized
UNION ALL
SELECT 'standard on-track pass', SUM(standard_gain)
FROM categorized
ORDER BY category

WITH driver_season_points AS (
    SELECT ds.driver_id, r.year, MAX(ds.points) AS total_points
    FROM driver_standings ds
    JOIN races r ON ds.race_id = r.race_id
    WHERE r.year >= 2001
    GROUP BY ds.driver_id, r.year
    HAVING total_points > 0
),
tdr_with_driver_id AS (
    SELECT tdr.year, tdr.constructor_ref, d.driver_id
    FROM team_driver_ranks tdr
    JOIN drivers d ON tdr.driver_ref = d.driver_ref
    WHERE tdr.year >= 2001
),
constructor_season_points AS (
    SELECT td.year, td.constructor_ref, SUM(dsp.total_points) AS constructor_total
    FROM tdr_with_driver_id td
    JOIN driver_season_points dsp ON td.driver_id = dsp.driver_id AND td.year = dsp.year
    GROUP BY td.year, td.constructor_ref
),
season_min AS (
    SELECT year, MIN(constructor_total) AS min_total
    FROM constructor_season_points
    GROUP BY year
),
min_constructors AS (
    SELECT csp.year, csp.constructor_ref
    FROM constructor_season_points csp
    JOIN season_min sm ON csp.year = sm.year AND csp.constructor_total = sm.min_total
),
constructor_counts AS (
    SELECT constructor_ref, COUNT(*) AS num_seasons
    FROM min_constructors
    GROUP BY constructor_ref
)
SELECT c.name, cc.num_seasons
FROM constructor_counts cc
JOIN constructors c ON cc.constructor_ref = c.constructor_ref
ORDER BY cc.num_seasons DESC
LIMIT 5
pred.local309:
WITH
-- Map driver_id to driver_ref from team_driver_ranks
driver_map AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT driver_id, driver_ref
    FROM team_driver_ranks
),
-- Total points per driver per year
driver_points AS (
    SELECT r.year, res.driver_id, SUM(res.points) AS total_points
    FROM results res
    JOIN races r ON res.race_id = r.race_id
    GROUP BY r.year, res.driver_id
),
-- Total points per constructor per year
constructor_points AS (
    SELECT r.year, res.constructor_id, SUM(res.points) AS total_points
    FROM results res
    JOIN races r ON res.race_id = r.race_id
    GROUP BY r.year, res.constructor_id
),
-- Rank drivers per year
ranked_drivers AS (
    SELECT year, driver_id, total_points,
           RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY total_points DESC) AS rnk
    FROM driver_points
),
-- Rank constructors per year
ranked_constructors AS (
    SELECT year, constructor_id, total_points,
           RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY total_points DESC) AS rnk
    FROM constructor_points
)
SELECT rd.year, de.full_name AS top_driver, ce.name AS top_constructor
FROM ranked_drivers rd
JOIN driver_map dm ON rd.driver_id = dm.driver_id
JOIN drivers_ext de ON dm.driver_ref = de.driver_ref
JOIN ranked_constructors rc ON rd.year = rc.year AND rc.rnk = 1
JOIN constructors_ext ce ON rc.constructor_id = ce.constructor_id
WHERE rd.rnk = 1
ORDER BY rd.year

WITH driver_yearly AS (

        r.year,
        res.driver_id,
        SUM(res.points) AS total_points

    GROUP BY r.year, res.driver_id
),
max_driver_per_year AS (

    FROM driver_yearly
    GROUP BY year
),
constructor_yearly AS (

        r.year,

),
max_constructor_per_year AS (

    FROM constructor_yearly

),
yearly_sum AS (

        d.year,
        d.max_driver_points + c.max_constructor_points AS total
    FROM max_driver_per_year d

WITH driver_points_per_year AS (
    -- Assume driver_standings has a race_id column (common in racing DBs)
    SELECT ds.driver_id, r.year, SUM(ds.points) AS total_points
    FROM driver_standings ds
    JOIN races r ON ds.race_id = r.race_id
    GROUP BY ds.driver_id, r.year
),
constructor_points_per_year AS (
    SELECT cs.constructor_id, r.year, SUM(cs.points) AS total_points
    FROM constructor_standings cs
    JOIN races r ON cs.race_id = r.race_id
    GROUP BY cs.constructor_id, r.year
),
driver_team_year AS (
    SELECT tdr.constructor_id, tdr.driver_id, tdr.year
    FROM team_driver_ranks tdr
),
best_driver_per_constructor_year AS (
    SELECT dty.constructor_id, dty.year, MAX(dpy.total_points) AS best_driver_points
    FROM driver_team_year dty
    JOIN driver_points_per_year dpy ON dty.driver_id = dpy.driver_id AND dty.year = dpy.year
    GROUP BY dty.constructor_id, dty.year
),
combined_points AS (
    SELECT
        bd.constructor_id,
        bd.year,
        (bd.best_driver_points + COALESCE(cpy.total_points, 0)) AS combined
    FROM best_driver_per_constructor_year bd
    LEFT JOIN constructor_points_per_year cpy ON bd.constructor_id = cpy.constructor_id AND bd.year = cpy.year
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        constructor_id,
        year,
        combined,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY combined DESC) AS rnk
    FROM combined_points
)
SELECT c.name AS constructor, r.year
FROM ranked r
JOIN constructors c ON r.constructor_id = c.constructor_id
WHERE r.rnk <= 3
ORDER BY r.rnk

WITH driver_years AS (
    -- Drivers who raced in the 1950s and drove for exactly one constructor in a year (from team_driver_ranks)
    SELECT driver_id, year
    FROM team_driver_ranks
    WHERE year BETWEEN 1950 AND 1959
    GROUP BY driver_id, year
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT constructor_id) = 1
),
race_participation AS (
    -- All races in the 1950s with driver participation
    SELECT res.driver_id, ra.year, ra.race_id, ra.round
    FROM results res
    JOIN races ra ON res.race_id = ra.race_id
    WHERE ra.year BETWEEN 1950 AND 1959
),
season_races AS (
    -- Drivers who participated in at least 2 distinct rounds in a year
    SELECT driver_id, year, COUNT(DISTINCT round) AS num_races
    FROM race_participation
    GROUP BY driver_id, year
    HAVING num_races >= 2
)
SELECT DISTINCT d.forename, d.surname
FROM driver_years dy
JOIN season_races sr ON dy.driver_id = sr.driver_id AND dy.year = sr.year
JOIN drivers d ON dy.driver_id = d.driver_id
ORDER BY d.surname, d.forename

WITH active_drivers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT driver_id, r.year
    FROM results
    JOIN races r ON results.race_id = r.race_id
),
all_driver_year_races AS (
    SELECT ad.driver_id, ad.year, r.round, r.race_id,
           res.constructor_id,
           CASE WHEN res.race_id IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_missing
    FROM active_drivers ad
    JOIN races r ON ad.year = r.year
    LEFT JOIN results res ON res.race_id = r.race_id AND res.driver_id = ad.driver_id
),
missed_totals AS (
    SELECT *,
           SUM(is_missing) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year) AS total_missed_in_year
    FROM all_driver_year_races
),
lag_lead AS (
    SELECT *,
           LAG(constructor_id) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) AS prev_constructor,
           LEAD(constructor_id) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) AS next_constructor
    FROM missed_totals
    WHERE total_missed_in_year < 3
),
missing_with_groups AS (
    SELECT *,
           CASE WHEN is_missing = 1 AND 
                     (LAG(is_missing,1,0) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) = 0)
                THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_start
    FROM lag_lead
),
missing_groups AS (
    SELECT *,
           SUM(is_start) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year ORDER BY round) AS grp
    FROM missing_with_groups
    WHERE is_missing = 1
),
numbered_groups AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year, grp ORDER BY round) AS rn_in_group,
           COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id, year, grp) AS group_size
    FROM missing_groups
),
gap_details AS (
    SELECT driver_id, year, grp,
           MIN(round) AS first_round,
           MAX(round) AS last_round,
           MAX(CASE WHEN rn_in_group = 1 THEN prev_constructor END) AS constructor_before,
           MAX(CASE WHEN rn_in_group = group_size THEN next_constructor END) AS constructor_after
    FROM numbered_groups
    GROUP BY driver_id, year, grp
)
SELECT 
    AVG(first_round) AS avg_first_round_of_missed,
    AVG(last_round) AS avg_last_round_of_missed
FROM gap_details
WHERE constructor_before IS NOT NULL
  AND constructor_after IS NOT NULL
  AND constructor_before != constructor_after

WITH race_laps AS (
    SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap, position
    FROM lap_positions
    WHERE lap_type = 'Race'
),
pit_laps AS (
    SELECT race_id, driver_id, lap
    FROM pit_stops
),
changes AS (
    SELECT r1.driver_id,
           r1.race_id,
           r1.lap AS from_lap,
           r1.position AS old_pos,
           r2.position AS new_pos
    FROM race_laps r1
    JOIN race_laps r2
        ON r1.race_id = r2.race_id
        AND r1.driver_id = r2.driver_id
        AND r1.lap = r2.lap - 1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM pit_laps p
        WHERE p.race_id = r1.race_id
          AND p.driver_id = r1.driver_id
          AND (p.lap = r1.lap OR p.lap = r2.lap)
    )
)
SELECT d.full_name
FROM (
    SELECT driver_id,
           SUM(CASE WHEN new_pos > old_pos THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overtaken,
           SUM(CASE WHEN new_pos < old_pos THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overtakes
    FROM changes
    GROUP BY driver_id
    HAVING overtaken > overtakes
) AS stats
JOIN drivers_ext d ON stats.driver_id = d.driver_id

WITH population_data AS (
    SELECT 
        ts.date,
        ts.geo_id,
        ts.value AS population_estimate,
        LAG(ts.value) OVER (PARTITION BY ts.geo_id ORDER BY ts.date) AS prev_year_population
    FROM GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.AMERICAN_COMMUNITY_SURVEY_TIMESERIES ts
    JOIN GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.AMERICAN_COMMUNITY_SURVEY_ATTRIBUTES att
        ON ts.variable = att.variable
    WHERE att.measure = 'Population'
        AND att.measurement_period = '5-Year'
        AND ts.date BETWEEN '2015-01-01' AND '2020-12-31'
        AND ts.value IS NOT NULL
        AND ts.value >= 25000
),
growth_rates AS (
    SELECT 
        date,
        geo_id,
        population_estimate,
        prev_year_population,
        (population_estimate - prev_year_population) / prev_year_population * 100 AS growth_rate_pct
    FROM population_data
    WHERE prev_year_population IS NOT NULL
        AND prev_year_population > 0
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        date,
        geo_id,
        growth_rate_pct,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY date ORDER BY growth_rate_pct DESC) AS rn
    FROM growth_rates
)
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM r.date) AS year,
    r.geo_id AS zip_code,
    s.state_abbreviation AS state_abbreviation,
    ROUND(r.growth_rate_pct, 2) AS annual_growth_rate_pct
FROM ranked r
JOIN GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.GEOGRAPHY_INDEX g
    ON r.geo_id = g.geo_id
JOIN GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.GLOBAL_GOVERNMENT.CYBERSYN.STATE_INDEX s
    ON g.state_geo_id = s.state_geo_id
WHERE r.rn = 2
ORDER BY year

WITH home_depot AS (

        poi.poi_id,
        addr.latitude AS lat,
        addr.longitude AS lon
    FROM US_REAL_ESTATE.CYBERSYN.POINT_OF_INTEREST_INDEX poi
    JOIN US_REAL_ESTATE.CYBERSYN.POINT_OF_INTEREST_ADDRESSES_RELATIONSHIPS rel
        ON poi.poi_id = rel.poi_id
    JOIN US_REAL_ESTATE.CYBERSYN.US_ADDRESSES addr
        ON rel.address_id = addr.address_id
    WHERE poi.poi_name = 'The Home Depot'
),
lowes AS (
    SELECT 
        poi.poi_id,
        addr.latitude AS lat,
        addr.longitude AS lon
    FROM US_REAL_ESTATE.CYBERSYN.POINT_OF_INTEREST_INDEX poi
    JOIN US_REAL_ESTATE.CYBERSYN.POINT_OF_INTEREST_ADDRESSES_RELATIONSHIPS rel
        ON poi.poi_id = rel.poi_id
    JOIN US_REAL_ESTATE.CYBERSYN.US_ADDRESSES addr

    WHERE poi.poi_name = 'Lowe''s Home Improvement'
)
SELECT 
    hd.poi_id AS home_depot_poi_id,
    ST_DISTANCE(
        ST_MAKEPOINT(hd.lon, hd.lat),
        ST_MAKEPOINT(lw.lon, lw.lat)
    ) / 1609.344 AS distance_miles
FROM home_depot hd
CROSS JOIN lowes lw
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY hd.poi_id ORDER BY ST_DISTANCE(
        ST_MAKEPOINT(hd.lon, hd.lat),
        ST_MAKEPOINT(lw.lon, lw.lat)
    )) = 1

),
net_ppm_data AS (

    WHERE DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW = 'Manufacturing'

SELECT

    s.PROGRAM,
    s.PERIOD,
    s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW,

    CASE WHEN s.ORDERED_UNITS > 0 THEN s.ORDERED_REVENUE / s.ORDERED_UNITS ELSE NULL END AS avg_selling_price,

    (tf_load.value - COALESCE(tf_wind_forecast.value, 0) - COALESCE(tf_solar_forecast.value, 0)) AS net_load_forecast,
    (rl.load - COALESCE(aw_wind_actual.actual_wind_speed_mph, 0) - COALESCE(aw_solar_actual.actual_dry_bulb_temp_f, 0)) AS net_load_real_time
FROM YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.ISO_MARKET_TIMES_SAMPLE imt
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.DART_PRICES_SAMPLE dp
    ON imt.iso = dp.iso
    AND imt.datetime = dp.datetime
    AND dp.objectid = 10000697078
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE tf_load
    ON imt.iso = tf_load.timezone
    AND imt.datetime = tf_load.datetime
    AND tf_load.datatypeid = 19060
    AND tf_load.objectid = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.RT_LOADS_SAMPLE rl
    ON imt.iso = rl.timezone
    AND imt.datetime = rl.datetime
    AND rl.objectid = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE tf_wind_forecast
    ON imt.iso = tf_wind_forecast.timezone
    AND imt.datetime = tf_wind_forecast.datetime
    AND tf_wind_forecast.datatypeid = 9285
    AND tf_wind_forecast.objectid = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.ALL_WEATHER_MV_SAMPLE aw_wind_actual
    ON imt.iso = aw_wind_actual.timezone
    AND imt.datetime = aw_wind_actual.datetime
    AND aw_wind_actual.objectid = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE tf_solar_forecast
    ON imt.iso = tf_solar_forecast.timezone
    AND imt.datetime = tf_solar_forecast.datetime
    AND tf_solar_forecast.datatypeid = 662
    AND tf_solar_forecast.objectid = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.YES_ENERGY_SAMPLE.YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.ALL_WEATHER_MV_SAMPLE aw_solar_actual
    ON imt.iso = aw_solar_actual.timezone
    AND imt.datetime = aw_solar_actual.datetime
    AND aw_solar_actual.objectid = 10000712973
WHERE imt.iso = 'ERCOT'
    AND imt.marketday = '2022-10-01'
ORDER BY imt.datetime

WITH tract_population AS (
    SELECT
        dg.StateCountyTractID,
        SUM(fv.CensusValue) AS TotalTractPopulation
    FROM CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Fact_CensusValues_ACS2021 fv
    JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Dim_CensusGeography dg
        ON fv.BlockGroupID = dg.BlockGroupID
    JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Dim_CensusMetrics dm
        ON fv.MetricID = dm.MetricID
    WHERE dm.MetricTableID = 'S0101'
        AND dm.Descriptor1 = 'Total Population'
        AND dg.StateAbbrev = 'NY'
    GROUP BY dg.StateCountyTractID
)
SELECT
    fv.BlockGroupID,
    fv.CensusValue AS BlockGroupPopulation,
    dg.StateCountyTractID,
    tp.TotalTractPopulation,
    fv.CensusValue / NULLIF(tp.TotalTractPopulation, 0) AS PopulationRatio
FROM CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Fact_CensusValues_ACS2021 fv
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Dim_CensusGeography dg
    ON fv.BlockGroupID = dg.BlockGroupID
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Dim_CensusMetrics dm
    ON fv.MetricID = dm.MetricID
JOIN tract_population tp
    ON dg.StateCountyTractID = tp.StateCountyTractID
WHERE dm.MetricTableID = 'S0101'
    AND dm.Descriptor1 = 'Total Population'
    AND dg.StateAbbrev = 'NY'
ORDER BY dg.StateCountyTractID, fv.BlockGroupID

SELECT 
    f.CensusValueByZip AS total_commuters,
    f.ZipCode,
    s.StateBenchmarkValue,
    s.TotalStatePopulation
FROM CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.Fact_CensusValues_ACS2021_ByZip f
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.Dim_CensusMetrics m
    ON f.MetricID = m.MetricID
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.Fact_StateBenchmark_ACS2021 s
    ON f.MetricID = s.MetricID
    AND s.StateAbbrev = 'NY'
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.PUBLIC.CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.LU_GeographyExpanded g
    ON f.ZipCode = g.ZipCode
WHERE m.MetricTableID = 'S0801'
    AND m.Descriptor1 = 'COMMUTING TIME'
    AND m.Descriptor2 = '60+ minutes'
    AND g.PreferredStateAbbrev = 'NY'
ORDER BY f.CensusValueByZip DESC
LIMIT 1
